items.title: refactor: drop stopwords
items.body:
## Summary

- `_match_lang` raises `ValueError` when the closest available locale exceeds distance 10, instead of silently returning `None`. Uses `100000` as the initial `best_dist` sentinel so the loop always finds the true minimum before applying the threshold.
- Stopwords removed entirely — they had no unit tests justifying their existence and were papering over a flaw in the double-negative detector.
- Double-negative detection replaced with a proximity regex allowing at most one intervening token (e.g. `"not a lie"`, `"não é mentira"`), fixing the underlying issue that stopwords were working around.
- `test_unsupported_language` updated to expect `ValueError`.

## Test plan

- [ ] `python -m pytest tests/` — 11 passed, 111 subtests

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

items.title: Modernize for current Python (3.9+), drop legacy deps
items.body:
## Summary
- Remove `fabulous`, `future`, and `pillow` dependencies — now runs on the Python standard library only
- Add `compat.py` as a lightweight replacement for fabulous (ANSI colors, terminal info, text rendering)
- Drop Python 2 support, require Python 3.9+
- Add `pyproject.toml` and `__main__.py` for modern packaging (`python -m terminal_quest`)
- Fix level 4 directory reference bug, replace `old_div` with `//`, stabilize `random.sample` call
- Update README with current installation instructions

## Test plan
- [ ] Run `python -m terminal_quest` and play through all 10 levels
- [ ] Verify banner and color output display correctly in terminal
- [ ] Test `pip install .` in a fresh venv on Python 3.9+
- [ ] Confirm no third-party packages are required

items.title: Drop pydantic dependency, use stdlib dataclasses
items.body:
## Summary

- Replace pydantic `BaseModel` with stdlib `@dataclass` for `RiskScore`, `RiskEvent`, and `Session` models — pydantic was only used for typed data containers with no runtime validation that wasn't already handled by the calculation logic
- Eliminate the entire dependency bootstrap problem (venv creation, pip/uv install in setup.sh, import guards in hook_handler and CLI) — RTFI now has **zero third-party dependencies**, matching the pattern used by other Claude Code plugins
- Add `.mise.toml` to pin project Python version (3.14), so `mise install` is all that's needed for environment setup

## Motivation

The only third-party dependency (`pydantic>=2.0.0`) caused a cascading setup problem: hooks needed a venv, setup.sh needed to create the venv before the CLI could run, and macOS system Python (3.9.6) couldn't install packages without elevated permissions. Other Claude Code plugins (e.g. token-optimizer) avoid this entirely by using stdlib only.

## Changes

| Area | Change |
|------|--------|
| `scripts/rtfi_core.py` | `BaseModel` → `@dataclass`, `Field()` → `field()`, `.model_dump()` → `asdict()` |
| `scripts/rtfi_dashboard.py` | Same `.model_dump()` → `asdict()` fix (also fixes a pre-existing test failure) |
| `scripts/hook_handler.py` | Removed pydantic import guard |
| `scripts/rtfi_cli.py` | Removed pydantic import guard, setup check, and health check |
| `scripts/setup.sh` | Removed venv creation and pydantic install; validates Python version and runs CLI setup |
| `scripts/run_hook.sh` | Removed venv fallback, just calls `python3` directly |
| `.mise.toml` | New — pins `python = "3.14"` |
| `pyproject.toml` | `dependencies = []` |
| CI/Makefile/marketplace | Removed pydantic from all install commands |
| README/docs | Updated install instructions, troubleshooting, architecture |

## Test plan

- [x] All 90 tests pass (`python3 -m pytest tests/ -v`)
- [ ] Verify `bash scripts/setup.sh` works on a clean machine with mise
- [ ] Verify `/rtfi:status` and `/rtfi:health` work without pydantic installed
- [ ] Verify dashboard launches and session detail API returns valid JSON

items.title: simplify-architecture: collapse daemon into plugin, drop systemd
items.body:
## Summary

Follow-up to [PR #1](https://github.com/MaxInertia/claude-voice-input-channel/pull/1) (review-fixes). That PR added a systemd layer to manage the separate `voice-sttd` and `voice-stt-ptt` services. This PR deletes that whole layer — the daemon is now spawned as a Python subprocess of the TS channel plugin, tied to the Claude Code session lifetime.

The multi-session VRAM argument that justified the three-process architecture turned out to be a phantom requirement (we confirmed this in conversation: one user, one mic, one PTT button, never two sessions competing for voice). Collapsing trades a small model-reload cost (~2–3 seconds on each fresh `claude` launch) for a dramatically simpler install flow that matches voicemode's 3-command ergonomics:

```
/plugin marketplace add MaxInertia/claude-voice-input-channel
/plugin install voice-stt@voice-stt-local
/voice-stt:install
```

Net LOC delta: **+1,750 / −1,285 ≈ +465**, but 663 of those added lines are the new design plan document itself. Subtracting the plan, the actual code+docs change is **~−200 net** — the refactor is strictly deletion-heavy.

## Phases (each commit is self-contained)

| # | Commit | Change |
|---|---|---|
| plan | `d1568fe` | Write the design plan, with feedback from plannotator |
| 1 | `32be8d7` | `daemon.py` speaks stdio: start/stop on stdin, transcripts on stdout, logs on stderr. Delete ctrl/out socket serving code. |
| 2 | `64d19f0` | PTT listener becomes a thread inside `voice-sttd`. Direct method calls to `start_recording()`/`stop_recording()`, no IPC. |
| 3 | `19a809f` | `src/voice_stt/config.py` loads `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/voice-stt/config`. Shell env wins over config file. `plugin/config.template` ships as the bundled default. |
| 4 | `66f3861` | `plugin/channel/voice-stt-channel.ts` rewritten to `Bun.spawn('uv', 'run', 'voice-sttd')` and forward stdout→`notifications/claude/channel`. `src/voice_stt/singleton.py` uses `fcntl.flock` on `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/voice-stt/daemon.lock` to prevent concurrent instances, with exit code 2 as a permanent-contention sentinel that bypasses the TS restart loop. |
| 5 | `cf9485b` | `plugin/commands/install.md` — `/voice-stt:install` slash command that runs prereq checks, `uv sync`, `bun install`, creates the XDG config from template, migrates legacy `.env` and systemd units if detected. |
| 6 | `6c5b5b7` | Delete obsolete plumbing: `scripts/voice-stt-svc`, `scripts/claude-voice`, `scripts/install-systemd-units`, `scripts/systemd/`, `scripts/xbindkeysrc.example`, `src/voice_stt/client.py`, `src/voice_stt/ptt_listener.py`, `.env.example`. `scripts/` directory gone entirely. |
| 7 | `83ea0f9` | README rewritten around the new 3-command install. Old plan doc gets a retrospective pointer. Obsidian vault setup doc updated with the new architecture diagram. |

## What's preserved

The architectural cleanups are limited to the lifecycle/packaging layer. All the correctness work from PR #1 is unchanged:

- **Segment join** (one PTT press = one transcript = one channel event)
- **PTT rescan-on-device-disappearance** (survives input-remapper reload)
- **Broadcast lock scoping** (though `_broadcast` itself is gone — transcripts go straight to stdout now)
- **Background transcription worker** (still serialized, still bounded queue, still drops oldest on overflow)
- **Persistent `sd.InputStream`** (prevents the open/close race and the virtual-source alternating-zero bug)
- **XDG_RUNTIME_DIR** usage — now just for the singleton lockfile, no longer for sockets
- **`_bridge_pulse_source`** — still bridges `VOICE_STT_PULSE_SOURCE` → `PULSE_SOURCE` before PortAudio opens the pulse backend, now runs after `config.load()` instead of being driven by `voice-stt-svc`'s bash
- **Prompt-injection caveats** in docs
- **Hardware PTT wiring** via input-remapper → `KEY_F20` — unchanged

## Testing

Smoke tests run autonomously during development (systemd legacy services were stopped for each test so the mic and singleton lock were free):

### Phase 1: daemon over stdio
```
$ timeout 10 bash -c '(sleep 3; echo) | uv run voice-sttd 2>&1'
[voice-sttd] loading model=medium.en device=cuda compute=float16
[voice-sttd] model ready
[voice-sttd] opening input device: 'pulse' (PULSE_SOURCE='<unset>')
[voice-sttd] audio input stream open (persistent)
[voice-sttd] ready; awaiting stdin commands
[voice-sttd] bye
```

### Phase 2: PTT thread inside daemon
```
[voice-sttd] PTT: listening for KEY_F20 on 6 devices
[voice-sttd] PTT:   - /dev/input/event12  input-remapper Razer Razer Naga V2 HyperSpeed forwarded
[voice-sttd] PTT:   - /dev/input/event2  input-remapper keyboard
...
```
Thread spins up, enumerates all 6 watched devices via `_find_keyboards`, same code path as the old standalone `voice-stt-ptt`.

### Phase 3: XDG config loaded and honored
```
$ printf 'VOICE_STT_MODEL=small.en\nVOICE_STT_PULSE_SOURCE=alsa_input.pci-0000_09_00.4.analog-stereo\n' > /tmp/xdg-test/voice-stt/config
$ XDG_CONFIG_HOME=/tmp/xdg-test timeout 10 bash -c '(sleep 3; echo) | uv run voice-sttd 2>&1'
[voice-sttd] loaded 2 setting(s) from /tmp/xdg-test/voice-stt/config
[voice-sttd] loading model=small.en device=cuda compute=float16
[voice-sttd] opening input device: 'pulse' (PULSE_SOURCE='alsa_input.pci-0000_09_00.4.analog-stereo')
```
Model override honored, PULSE_SOURCE bridged post-config-load.

### Phase 4: singleton lock
```
$ ( (sleep 5; echo) | uv run voice-sttd 2>&1 ) &
$ sleep 3.5; echo | uv run voice-sttd 2>&1; echo "second exit=$?"
[voice-sttd] another voice-stt daemon is already running (lock held on
/run/user/1000/voice-stt/daemon.lock, previous pid: 306038).
second exit=2
```
First daemon holds the lock; second exits with code 2 immediately. Lockfile content is the holding pid for diagnostics.

### Phase 6+7: post-deletion full lifecycle
With everything deleted and the real `~/.config/voice-stt/config` in place (migrated from the repo-root `.env`):
```
$ timeout 10 bash -c '(sleep 4; echo) | uv run voice-sttd 2>&1 | grep -E "loaded|loading|opening|PTT: listening"'
[voice-sttd] loaded 6 setting(s) from /home/maxinertia/.config/voice-stt/config
[voice-sttd] loading model=medium.en device=cuda compute=float16
[voice-sttd] opening input device: 'pulse' (PULSE_SOURCE='alsa_input.pci-0000_09_00.4.analog-stereo')
[voice-sttd] PTT: listening for KEY_F20 on 6 devices
```
Config loaded from XDG path, model loaded, PULSE_SOURCE bridged, PTT thread online, clean shutdown on EOF.

## What I couldn't test autonomously

The Bun channel plugin's `Bun.spawn` → Python path, the full TS→Python stdio IPC, the restart-on-crash loop, and the `/voice-stt:install` slash command all require a fresh Claude Code session because:

1. The currently-running Bun MCP server (pid 264531) has the OLD cached channel code baked into memory — it's still reconnecting to the `/run/user/1000/voice-stt/out.sock` path that no longer exists
2. Refreshing the plugin cache doesn't restart the long-lived in-memory Bun process
3. `/voice-stt:install` only runs inside a Claude Code session, and running it in the CURRENT session would interrupt the work

**User needs to do after merging:**

```bash
# 1. Exit the current Claude Code session
#    (the running Bun plugin process will die)

# 2. In a fresh shell / fresh Claude Code launch, refresh the plugin cache:
/plugin marketplace update voice-stt-local
/plugin uninstall voice-stt@voice-stt-local
/plugin install voice-stt@voice-stt-local
/reload-plugins

# 3. Exit that session and relaunch with the channel flag:
claude --dangerously-load-development-channels plugin:voice-stt@voice-stt-local

# 4. Hold Naga `=` and dictate a test phrase
#    - Expect: <channel source="voice-stt" seq="1"> arrives within 2-3s
#    - Expect: no 'Noise Canceling source' alternating-silence regression
#    - Expect: /exit kills the Python child within ~2s (watch pgrep)
```

If anything in step 4 fails, check `~/.claude/debug/<session-id>.txt` for the channel server's stderr (which now forwards the Python daemon's stderr verbatim).

## Migration notes

- **Legacy systemd user units** from PR #1 are still enabled on the dev machine. `/voice-stt:install` detects them and offers to `systemctl --user disable --now` + remove the unit files. They can also be cleaned up manually.
- **The repo-root `.env`** from PR #1 was already migrated to `~/.config/voice-stt/config` during Phase 8 testing (the one `VOICE_STT_PULSE_SOURCE` override for bypassing the Noise Canceling source). The original `.env` file has been deleted from the working tree (was gitignored, not tracked).
- **The stale plugin cache** at `~/.claude/plugins/cache/voice-stt-local/` will auto-refresh on the next `/plugin install`.
- **No rollback plan** since PR #1 is already on master — if this PR needs to be reverted, `git revert` the merge commit and re-run `install-systemd-units` from the reverted tree. All the systemd infrastructure is in PR #1's history.

## Post-Deploy Monitoring & Validation

- **What to monitor** — `journalctl --user -u voice-sttd.service -f` before the branch merges (confirm the legacy services are cleanly stopped post-migration), `~/.claude/debug/<session-id>.txt` after the branch merges (all daemon logging lands there via the TS channel's stderr forwarder)
- **Validation checks** —
  - `systemctl --user is-active voice-sttd.service voice-stt-ptt.service` → should return `inactive inactive` after cleanup (or `Unit ... not found` after the unit files are deleted)
  - `pgrep -af 'voice-sttd|voice-stt-ptt'` during an active Claude Code session → should show exactly one Python process, child of a Bun process
  - `pgrep -af 'voice-sttd'` after `/exit` from Claude Code → should return nothing within 2 seconds
  - `ls -la $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/voice-stt/daemon.lock` → should exist only while a session is active
- **Expected healthy behavior** — Hold Naga `=`, speak, release → one `<channel source="voice-stt" seq="N">` event arrives in the session within ~1 second of release
- **Failure signal / rollback trigger** — If dictation arrives as alternating silent buffers OR if the daemon fails to start cleanly from the plugin path, investigate the PULSE_SOURCE config in `~/.config/voice-stt/config` first, then the debug log. Rollback is `git revert` the PR.
- **Validation window & owner** — First 3 days of daily use. If no issues by then, consider the refactor landed.

---

[![Compound Engineered](https://img.shields.io/badge/Compound-Engineered-6366f1)](https://github.com/EveryInc/compound-engineering-plugin) 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

items.title: drop YouTube transcript refs, fix Amazon country param, bump 2.4
items.body:
## Summary
- Remove leftover ScavioYouTubeTranscript references from the README (the tool was removed in df89bb4 but docs still shipped them).
- Switch the LangGraph agent example to the current \`langchain.agents.create_agent\` API.
- Update \`pyproject.toml\` description so the PyPI project page surfaces Amazon/Walmart/YouTube support; bump version 2.3 -> 2.4.
- Drop the misleading \`country\` field from \`ScavioAmazonSearch\` / \`ScavioAmazonProduct\` args schemas. It was not the Amazon marketplace selector and caused upstream 400s when agents passed values like \`"US"\`. \`domain\` is the only way to pick a store.
- Expand the \`domain\` description to enumerate all 22 supported marketplaces (com, co.uk, ca, de, fr, es, it, co.jp, in, com.au, com.br, com.mx, nl, pl, se, sg, ae, sa, eg, cn, com.be, com.tr).

## Test plan
- [x] Full pytest suite green locally (144 passing)
- [ ] CI green across Python 3.10-3.13
- [ ] After merge: publish 2.4 to PyPI and verify the new description on the project page

items.title: chore(deps): bump python-dotenv from 1.2.1 to 1.2.2
items.body:
Bumps [python-dotenv](https://github.com/theskumar/python-dotenv) from 1.2.1 to 1.2.2.
Release notes

Sourced from python-dotenv's releases.

v1.2.2

Added

  • Support for Python 3.14, including the free-threaded (3.14t) build. (#)

Changed

  • The dotenv run command now forwards flags directly to the specified command by @​bbc2 in theskumar/python-dotenv#607
  • Improved documentation clarity regarding override behavior and the reference page.
  • Updated PyPy support to version 3.11.
  • Documentation for FIFO file support.
  • Support for Python 3.9.

Fixed

  • Improved set_key and unset_key behavior when interacting with symlinks by @​bbc2 in #790c5
  • Corrected the license specifier and added missing Python 3.14 classifiers in package metadata by @​JYOuyang in theskumar/python-dotenv#590

Breaking Changes

  • dotenv.set_key and dotenv.unset_key used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case. For that behavior to be restored in all cases, follow_symlinks=True should be used.

  • In the CLI, set and unset used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case.

  • dotenv.set_key, dotenv.unset_key and the CLI commands set and unset used to reset the file mode of the modified .env file to 0o600 in some situations. This is no longer the case: The original mode of the file is now preserved. Is the file needed to be created or wasn't a regular file, mode 0o600 is used.

Misc

  • skip 000 permission tests for root user by @​burnout-projects in theskumar/python-dotenv#561
  • Bump actions/checkout from 5 to 6 in the github-actions group by @​dependabot[bot] in theskumar/python-dotenv#593
  • Add Windows testing to CI by @​bbc2 in theskumar/python-dotenv#604
  • Improve workflow efficiency with best practices by @​theskumar in theskumar/python-dotenv#609
  • Remove the use of sh in tests by @​bbc2 in theskumar/python-dotenv#612

New Contributors

  • @​JYOuyang made their first contribution in theskumar/python-dotenv#590
  • @​burnout-projects made their first contribution in theskumar/python-dotenv#561
  • @​cpackham-atlnz made their first contribution in theskumar/python-dotenv#597

Full Changelog: https://github.com/theskumar/python-dotenv/compare/v1.2.1...v1.2.2

Changelog

Sourced from python-dotenv's changelog.

[1.2.2] - 2026-03-01

Added

  • Support for Python 3.14, including the free-threaded (3.14t) build. (#588)

Changed

  • The dotenv run command now forwards flags directly to the specified command by [@​bbc2] in #607
  • Improved documentation clarity regarding override behavior and the reference page.
  • Updated PyPy support to version 3.11.
  • Documentation for FIFO file support.
  • Dropped Support for Python 3.9.

Fixed

  • Improved set_key and unset_key behavior when interacting with symlinks by [@​bbc2] in [790c5c0]
  • Corrected the license specifier and added missing Python 3.14 classifiers in package metadata by [@​JYOuyang] in #590

Breaking Changes

  • dotenv.set_key and dotenv.unset_key used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case. For that behavior to be restored in all cases, follow_symlinks=True should be used.

  • In the CLI, set and unset used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case.

  • dotenv.set_key, dotenv.unset_key and the CLI commands set and unset used to reset the file mode of the modified .env file to 0o600 in some situations. This is no longer the case: The original mode of the file is now preserved. Is the file needed to be created or wasn't a regular file, mode 0o600 is used.

Commits
  • 36004e0 Bump version: 1.2.1 → 1.2.2
  • eb20252 docs: update changelog for v1.2.2
  • 790c5c0 Merge commit from fork
  • 43340da Remove the use of sh in tests (#612)
  • 09d7cee docs: clarify override behavior and document FIFO support (#610)
  • c8de288 ci: improve workflow efficiency with best practices (#609)
  • 7bd9e3d Add Windows testing to CI (#604)
  • 1baaf04 Drop Python 3.9 support and update to PyPy 3.11 (#608)
  • 4a22cf8 ci: enable testing on Python 3.14t (free-threaded) (#588)
  • e2e8e77 Fix license specifier (#597)
  • Additional commits viewable in compare view

[![Dependabot compatibility score](https://dependabot-badges.githubapp.com/badges/compatibility_score?dependency-name=python-dotenv&package-manager=uv&previous-version=1.2.1&new-version=1.2.2)](https://docs.github.com/en/github/managing-security-vulnerabilities/about-dependabot-security-updates#about-compatibility-scores) Dependabot will resolve any conflicts with this PR as long as you don't alter it yourself. items.title: Add offline notebook mode, drop Python 2.6, bump to v1.7.0
items.body:
## Summary
- Add `plotly.offline` module with `download_plotlyjs()`, `init_notebook_mode()`, and `iplot()` for rendering figures locally in IPython/Jupyter without requiring Plotly server access
- Export link text automatically follows session `plotly_domain` when set to a non-default value
- Add core tests (error paths without IPython) and optional tests (IPython-dependent happy paths) following existing test conventions
- Remove Python 2.6 from CircleCI config/scripts, setup.py classifiers, and extras_require; delete `optional-requirements-2-6.txt`
- Bump version to 1.7.0
- Update setup.py URL to `https://plot.ly/python/`

## Test plan
- [x] Core offline tests pass (8 tests): bundle missing errors, init-required errors, domain logic
- [x] Optional offline tests pass (8 tests): init_notebook_mode sets flag, iplot accepts dict/list, rejects invalid input, handles show_link=False, custom link_text, custom domain, preserves user link_text with custom domain
- [ ] Verify CI runs cleanly without Python 2.6 targets

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

items.body:
Bumps [python-dotenv](https://github.com/theskumar/python-dotenv) from 1.2.1 to 1.2.2.
Release notes

Sourced from python-dotenv's releases.

v1.2.2

Added

  • Support for Python 3.14, including the free-threaded (3.14t) build. (#)

Changed

  • The dotenv run command now forwards flags directly to the specified command by @​bbc2 in theskumar/python-dotenv#607
  • Improved documentation clarity regarding override behavior and the reference page.
  • Updated PyPy support to version 3.11.
  • Documentation for FIFO file support.
  • Support for Python 3.9.

Fixed

  • Improved set_key and unset_key behavior when interacting with symlinks by @​bbc2 in #790c5
  • Corrected the license specifier and added missing Python 3.14 classifiers in package metadata by @​JYOuyang in theskumar/python-dotenv#590

Breaking Changes

  • dotenv.set_key and dotenv.unset_key used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case. For that behavior to be restored in all cases, follow_symlinks=True should be used.

  • In the CLI, set and unset used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case.

  • dotenv.set_key, dotenv.unset_key and the CLI commands set and unset used to reset the file mode of the modified .env file to 0o600 in some situations. This is no longer the case: The original mode of the file is now preserved. Is the file needed to be created or wasn't a regular file, mode 0o600 is used.

Misc

  • skip 000 permission tests for root user by @​burnout-projects in theskumar/python-dotenv#561
  • Bump actions/checkout from 5 to 6 in the github-actions group by @​dependabot[bot] in theskumar/python-dotenv#593
  • Add Windows testing to CI by @​bbc2 in theskumar/python-dotenv#604
  • Improve workflow efficiency with best practices by @​theskumar in theskumar/python-dotenv#609
  • Remove the use of sh in tests by @​bbc2 in theskumar/python-dotenv#612

New Contributors

  • @​JYOuyang made their first contribution in theskumar/python-dotenv#590
  • @​burnout-projects made their first contribution in theskumar/python-dotenv#561
  • @​cpackham-atlnz made their first contribution in theskumar/python-dotenv#597

Full Changelog: https://github.com/theskumar/python-dotenv/compare/v1.2.1...v1.2.2

Changelog

Sourced from python-dotenv's changelog.

[1.2.2] - 2026-03-01

Added

  • Support for Python 3.14, including the free-threaded (3.14t) build. (#588)

Changed

  • The dotenv run command now forwards flags directly to the specified command by [@​bbc2] in #607
  • Improved documentation clarity regarding override behavior and the reference page.
  • Updated PyPy support to version 3.11.
  • Documentation for FIFO file support.
  • Dropped Support for Python 3.9.

Fixed

  • Improved set_key and unset_key behavior when interacting with symlinks by [@​bbc2] in [790c5c0]
  • Corrected the license specifier and added missing Python 3.14 classifiers in package metadata by [@​JYOuyang] in #590

Breaking Changes

  • dotenv.set_key and dotenv.unset_key used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case. For that behavior to be restored in all cases, follow_symlinks=True should be used.

  • In the CLI, set and unset used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case.

  • dotenv.set_key, dotenv.unset_key and the CLI commands set and unset used to reset the file mode of the modified .env file to 0o600 in some situations. This is no longer the case: The original mode of the file is now preserved. Is the file needed to be created or wasn't a regular file, mode 0o600 is used.

Commits
  • 36004e0 Bump version: 1.2.1 → 1.2.2
  • eb20252 docs: update changelog for v1.2.2
  • 790c5c0 Merge commit from fork
  • 43340da Remove the use of sh in tests (#612)
  • 09d7cee docs: clarify override behavior and document FIFO support (#610)
  • c8de288 ci: improve workflow efficiency with best practices (#609)
  • 7bd9e3d Add Windows testing to CI (#604)
  • 1baaf04 Drop Python 3.9 support and update to PyPy 3.11 (#608)
  • 4a22cf8 ci: enable testing on Python 3.14t (free-threaded) (#588)
  • e2e8e77 Fix license specifier (#597)
  • Additional commits viewable in compare view

items.title: Add pytest coverage and fix timezone compatibility for Python 3.10
items.body:
### Motivation
- Add automated test coverage for the API surface and message parsing logic to ensure behavior is verifiable.
- Fix Python 3.10 incompatibility where `datetime.UTC` was used, replacing it with `timezone.utc` so timestamps and startup inspection work correctly.

### Description
- Added test harness and fixtures in `tests/conftest.py` that set `PYTHONPATH`, provide a temporary SQLite `DATABASE_URL`, and recreate the schema per test.
- Added API integration tests in `tests/test_api.py` for the root endpoint, webhook ingestion/persistence, message listing, and invalid payload rejection.
- Added unit tests in `tests/test_message_service.py` covering numeric parsing and boolean coercion in `extract_payload_fields`.
- Replaced `from datetime import UTC, datetime` with `from datetime import datetime, timezone` and updated uses to `datetime.now(timezone.utc)` in `app/api/routes/admin.py` and `app/models/message.py`.

### Testing
- Ran `pytest -q` iteratively during development to resolve issues and confirm fixes, and the final test run completed with `4 passed` and `2 warnings`.
- Earlier automated test runs surfaced missing `PYTHONPATH` imports and the `UTC` import error which were fixed and re-validated by the subsequent `pytest` runs.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/tasks/task_e_69d657b03058832f9b6836703f558869)

items.title: Bump requests from 2.31.0 to 2.33.0

labels.name: dependencies
labels.description: Pull requests that update a dependency file

labels.name: python
labels.description: Pull requests that update python code

items.body:
Bumps [requests](https://github.com/psf/requests) from 2.31.0 to 2.33.0.
Release notes

Sourced from requests's releases.

v2.33.0

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • 📣 Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. 📣

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

Deprecations

  • Dropped support for Python 3.9 following its end of support. (#7196)

Documentation

  • Various typo fixes and doc improvements.

New Contributors

  • @​M0d3v1 made their first contribution in psf/requests#6865
  • @​aminvakil made their first contribution in psf/requests#7220
  • @​E8Price made their first contribution in psf/requests#6960
  • @​mitre88 made their first contribution in psf/requests#7244
  • @​magsen made their first contribution in psf/requests#6553
  • @​Rohan5commit made their first contribution in psf/requests#7227

Full Changelog: https://github.com/psf/requests/blob/main/HISTORY.md#2330-2026-03-25

v2.32.5

2.32.5 (2025-08-18)

Bugfixes

labels.description: Pull requests that update python code

items.body:
Bumps [psycopg2](https://github.com/psycopg/psycopg2) from 2.6.1 to 2.9.11.
Changelog

Sourced from psycopg2's changelog.

Current release

What's new in psycopg 2.9.11 ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

  • Add support for Python 3.14.
  • Avoid a segfault passing more arguments than placeholders if Python is built with assertions enabled (:ticket:[#1791](https://github.com/psycopg/psycopg2/issues/1791)).
  • Add riscv64 platform binary packages (:ticket:[#1813](https://github.com/psycopg/psycopg2/issues/1813)).
  • ~psycopg2.errorcodes map and ~psycopg2.errors classes updated to PostgreSQL 18.
  • Drop support for Python 3.8.

What's new in psycopg 2.9.10 ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

  • Add support for Python 3.13.
  • Receive notifications on commit (:ticket:[#1728](https://github.com/psycopg/psycopg2/issues/1728)).
  • ~psycopg2.errorcodes map and ~psycopg2.errors classes updated to PostgreSQL 17.
  • Drop support for Python 3.7.

What's new in psycopg 2.9.9 ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

  • Add support for Python 3.12.
  • Drop support for Python 3.6.

What's new in psycopg 2.9.8 ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

  • Wheel package bundled with PostgreSQL 16 libpq in order to add support for recent features, such as sslcertmode.

What's new in psycopg 2.9.7 ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

  • Fix propagation of exceptions raised during module initialization (:ticket:[#1598](https://github.com/psycopg/psycopg2/issues/1598)).
  • Fix building when pg_config returns an empty string (:ticket:[#1599](https://github.com/psycopg/psycopg2/issues/1599)).
  • Wheel package bundled with OpenSSL 1.1.1v.

What's new in psycopg 2.9.6 ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

items.title: Bump python-dotenv from 1.2.1 to 1.2.2

labels.name: dependencies
labels.description: Pull requests that update a dependency file

labels.name: python:uv
labels.description: Pull requests that update python:uv code

items.body:
Bumps [python-dotenv](https://github.com/theskumar/python-dotenv) from 1.2.1 to 1.2.2.
Release notes

Sourced from python-dotenv's releases.

v1.2.2

Added

  • Support for Python 3.14, including the free-threaded (3.14t) build. (#)

Changed

  • The dotenv run command now forwards flags directly to the specified command by @​bbc2 in theskumar/python-dotenv#607
  • Improved documentation clarity regarding override behavior and the reference page.
  • Updated PyPy support to version 3.11.
  • Documentation for FIFO file support.
  • Support for Python 3.9.

Fixed

  • Improved set_key and unset_key behavior when interacting with symlinks by @​bbc2 in #790c5
  • Corrected the license specifier and added missing Python 3.14 classifiers in package metadata by @​JYOuyang in theskumar/python-dotenv#590

Breaking Changes

  • dotenv.set_key and dotenv.unset_key used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case. For that behavior to be restored in all cases, follow_symlinks=True should be used.

  • In the CLI, set and unset used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case.

  • dotenv.set_key, dotenv.unset_key and the CLI commands set and unset used to reset the file mode of the modified .env file to 0o600 in some situations. This is no longer the case: The original mode of the file is now preserved. Is the file needed to be created or wasn't a regular file, mode 0o600 is used.

Misc

  • skip 000 permission tests for root user by @​burnout-projects in theskumar/python-dotenv#561
  • Bump actions/checkout from 5 to 6 in the github-actions group by @​dependabot[bot] in theskumar/python-dotenv#593
  • Add Windows testing to CI by @​bbc2 in theskumar/python-dotenv#604
  • Improve workflow efficiency with best practices by @​theskumar in theskumar/python-dotenv#609
  • Remove the use of sh in tests by @​bbc2 in theskumar/python-dotenv#612

New Contributors

  • @​JYOuyang made their first contribution in theskumar/python-dotenv#590
  • @​burnout-projects made their first contribution in theskumar/python-dotenv#561
  • @​cpackham-atlnz made their first contribution in theskumar/python-dotenv#597

Full Changelog: https://github.com/theskumar/python-dotenv/compare/v1.2.1...v1.2.2

Changelog

Sourced from python-dotenv's changelog.

[1.2.2] - 2026-03-01

Added

  • Support for Python 3.14, including the free-threaded (3.14t) build. (#588)

Changed

  • The dotenv run command now forwards flags directly to the specified command by [@​bbc2] in #607
  • Improved documentation clarity regarding override behavior and the reference page.
  • Updated PyPy support to version 3.11.
  • Documentation for FIFO file support.
  • Dropped Support for Python 3.9.

Fixed

  • Improved set_key and unset_key behavior when interacting with symlinks by [@​bbc2] in [790c5c0]
  • Corrected the license specifier and added missing Python 3.14 classifiers in package metadata by [@​JYOuyang] in #590

Breaking Changes

  • dotenv.set_key and dotenv.unset_key used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case. For that behavior to be restored in all cases, follow_symlinks=True should be used.

  • In the CLI, set and unset used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case.

  • dotenv.set_key, dotenv.unset_key and the CLI commands set and unset used to reset the file mode of the modified .env file to 0o600 in some situations. This is no longer the case: The original mode of the file is now preserved. Is the file needed to be created or wasn't a regular file, mode 0o600 is used.

Commits
  • 36004e0 Bump version: 1.2.1 → 1.2.2
  • eb20252 docs: update changelog for v1.2.2
  • 790c5c0 Merge commit from fork
  • 43340da Remove the use of sh in tests (#612)
  • 09d7cee docs: clarify override behavior and document FIFO support (#610)
  • c8de288 ci: improve workflow efficiency with best practices (#609)
  • 7bd9e3d Add Windows testing to CI (#604)
  • 1baaf04 Drop Python 3.9 support and update to PyPy 3.11 (#608)
  • 4a22cf8 ci: enable testing on Python 3.14t (free-threaded) (#588)
  • e2e8e77 Fix license specifier (#597)
  • Additional commits viewable in compare view

[![Dependabot compatibility score](https://dependabot-badges.githubapp.com/badges/compatibility_score?dependency-name=python-dotenv&package-manager=uv&previous-version=1.2.1&new-version=1.2.2)](https://docs.github.com/en/github/managing-security-vulnerabilities/about-dependabot-security-updates#about-compatibility-scores) Dependabot will resolve any conflicts with this PR as long as you don't alter it yourself. items.body:
Bumps [python-dotenv](https://github.com/theskumar/python-dotenv) from 1.0.1 to 1.2.2.
Release notes

Sourced from python-dotenv's releases.

v1.2.2

Added

  • Support for Python 3.14, including the free-threaded (3.14t) build. (#)

Changed

  • The dotenv run command now forwards flags directly to the specified command by @​bbc2 in theskumar/python-dotenv#607
  • Improved documentation clarity regarding override behavior and the reference page.
  • Updated PyPy support to version 3.11.
  • Documentation for FIFO file support.
  • Support for Python 3.9.

Fixed

  • Improved set_key and unset_key behavior when interacting with symlinks by @​bbc2 in #790c5
  • Corrected the license specifier and added missing Python 3.14 classifiers in package metadata by @​JYOuyang in theskumar/python-dotenv#590

Breaking Changes

  • dotenv.set_key and dotenv.unset_key used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case. For that behavior to be restored in all cases, follow_symlinks=True should be used.

  • In the CLI, set and unset used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case.

  • dotenv.set_key, dotenv.unset_key and the CLI commands set and unset used to reset the file mode of the modified .env file to 0o600 in some situations. This is no longer the case: The original mode of the file is now preserved. Is the file needed to be created or wasn't a regular file, mode 0o600 is used.

Misc

  • skip 000 permission tests for root user by @​burnout-projects in theskumar/python-dotenv#561
  • Bump actions/checkout from 5 to 6 in the github-actions group by @​dependabot[bot] in theskumar/python-dotenv#593
  • Add Windows testing to CI by @​bbc2 in theskumar/python-dotenv#604
  • Improve workflow efficiency with best practices by @​theskumar in theskumar/python-dotenv#609
  • Remove the use of sh in tests by @​bbc2 in theskumar/python-dotenv#612

New Contributors

  • @​JYOuyang made their first contribution in theskumar/python-dotenv#590
  • @​burnout-projects made their first contribution in theskumar/python-dotenv#561
  • @​cpackham-atlnz made their first contribution in theskumar/python-dotenv#597

Breaking Changes

  • dotenv.set_key and dotenv.unset_key used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case. For that behavior to be restored in all cases, follow_symlinks=True should be used.

items.title: Create faithful Python port of C inference engine, reorganize versions, update README
items.body:
The Python version (`postgpt_q.py`) diverged significantly from the canonical C implementation — different Dario coefficients, missing best-of-3 generation, no Schumann resonance, no memory persistence, dynamic chamber modulation instead of static, DOE alpha 2× stronger. Created a faithful 1:1 Python port and preserved the research version separately.

### Changes

- **`postgpt_q.py` → `qresearch.py`** — old research/training version (Val autograd, RoPE, wormholes, interference, dynamic Dario modulation) preserved under new name
- **New `postgpt_q.py`** (1449 lines) — faithful port of `postgpt_q.c`:
  - Same constants (`DOE_ALPHA=0.05`, `TOP_K=15`, `QPTQ_MAGIC=0x51505451`)
  - Same static Dario coefficients (`heb=0.4/0.8`, `bg=5.0/15.0` with/without weights)
  - Same pipeline: best-of-3 coherence scoring, greedy→nucleus hybrid, age-based repetition penalty, bigram blocking, Schumann resonance, `q.memory` persistence
  - No training, no autograd, no RoPE — pure inference matching C
- **`assets/`** — browser screenshots of `q.html` (start, generation, field view)
- **`README.md`** rewritten:
  - Author credit, versions table with canonical status
  - Feature comparison table (canonical vs research)
  - Inline browser screenshots
  - `.gitignore` updated for `__pycache__`

### Key differences resolved (C canonical → Python port)

| Feature | Old `postgpt_q.py` | New (matches C) |
|---------|-------------------|-----------------|
| Dario bigram coeff | 12.0 fixed | 5.0/15.0 (tf/no-tf) |
| DOE alpha | 0.1 | 0.05 |
| Best-of-3 | ❌ | ✅ |
| Schumann resonance | ❌ | ✅ |
| Memory persistence | ❌ | ✅ q.memory |
| Rep penalty | uniform 0.5 | age-based 0.3+0.035×age |

### Browser version screenshots

![start](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/797f8c0a-6a2d-4470-b90d-d95c775447e2)

![demo generation](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f3e2997f-1658-4d1d-837c-2b25116976d4)

![field view](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/169bd153-a5c2-4785-acea-09272ea114ae)

items.title: Feat/drop duplicates/ carlos henrique
items.body:
## 📌 O que foi feito?
Esta PR entrega a primeira funcionalidade real do nosso pipeline de limpeza: a identificação e remoção de registros idênticos na base de dados do Spotify.

## 🛠️ Detalhes Técnicos e Arquitetura
- **Performance no `drop_duplicates`:** Para evitar laços aninhados lentos, empacotei as linhas completas em tuplas (`tuple`). Como tuplas são imutáveis, pude utilizar a estrutura de dados `set` para verificar a existência de duplicatas em tempo constante $O(1)$, tornando a varredura extremamente rápida.
- **Sincronização de Estado:** Após a remoção das duplicatas, o dataset interno da classe `Preprocessing` é atualizado e propagado para as classes especialistas, garantindo que o resto do pipeline trabalhe apenas com dados limpos.
- **Testes Automatizados:** Inclusão do teste `test_drop_duplicates` no arquivo de testes unitários para garantir a estabilidade da função.

## 🧪 Como testar?
1. **Teste Prático:** Rode `python main.py`. Verifique se o console exibe a mensagem com o ícone de vassoura 🧹 informando a quantidade exata de linhas duplicadas removidas da base.
2. **Teste Unitário:** Rode `python -m unittest test_preprocessing.py`. O novo teste adicionado deve passar com sucesso (`OK`).

items.title: v0.3 Memory Matures: Tier 2 complete (F7-F12)
items.body:
## Summary

Ships the complete Tier 2 milestone in one drop — 6 architectural features, 178 new tests, 0 new pip deps, full backward compat.

| # | Feature | Key modules |
|---|---------|-------------|
| F7 | 4-tier memory consolidation w/ decay | `memory/consolidator.py`, `memory/decay.py` |
| F8 | Contradiction detection + supersession | `memory/contradiction.py`, `store.record_decision_checked` |
| F9 | Time-travel snapshots | `snapshots/manager.py`, `scripts/snapshot-guard.py` |
| F10 | Learned workspace profiles | `memory/profiler.py` |
| F11 | Cross-workspace pattern detection | `memory/pattern_detector.py`, `scripts/pattern-cron.py` |
| F12 | Inter-workspace signals (pub/sub) | `memory/signals.py` |

### Shared primitives (new)
`migration.py` (safe ALTER helper), `decay.py` (Ebbinghaus), `query_helpers.py` (cosine + top-k), `EmbeddingService.embed_batch_from_db`.

### Schema changes (all backward compatible)
- `sessions.memory_tier` (working/episodic)
- `decisions.contradiction_score`
- new `semantic_memories`, `procedural_memories` tables
- new `signals` table in `meta.db`
- All via `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN` + OperationalError catch; existing dbs migrate on first open.

### Integration
- **session-preamble**: snapshot on auto-switch; injects semantic facts, workspace profile, pending signals
- **session-end**: `maybe_compact()`, profile regen when stale, fires `pattern-cron.py` weekly
- **memory-bridge**: new `signal` / `read_signals` event types; decision handler now uses `record_decision_checked`
- **hooks.json**: new PreToolUse matcher for `Write` -> `snapshot-guard.py`

### New user skills
`compact-memory`, `decision-history`, `rollback`, `profile-refresh`, `cross-patterns`, `emit-signal`

### Test results
60 -> **238 passing, 0 failing**. Zero regressions.

### Build method
Multi-agent parallel build: Wave 1 (foundation) sequential -> Wave 2 (6 features in parallel) -> Wave 3 (sequential integration) -> Wave 4 (verify + ship).

## Test plan
- [x] `python -m pytest tests/ -q` -> 238 passed
- [x] `python -c "import json; json.load(open('hooks/hooks.json'))"` valid
- [x] All new modules import cleanly
- [ ] Human smoke: run a real session, verify PreCompact + snapshot + signal flows end-to-end
- [ ] Verify migration on an existing v0.2 memory.db (auto ALTER should be a no-op on re-open)

## Follow-ups (not in this PR)
- Tier 3 features (F13-F20) — session threads, billing, weekly summaries, environments, vault, deploy linking, inbox, NL router
- Port v0.3 into the local runtime `C:\Tentaqles\.tentaqles\` (still pinned to v0.2)
- LLM-based contradiction confirmation (full scope of F8)
- Weekly cron scheduling for `pattern-cron.py` and `compaction-cron.py`

items.title: chore(deps): update dependency python-dotenv to v1.2.2
items.body:
This PR contains the following updates:

| Package | Update | Change |
|---|---|---|
| [python-dotenv](https://redirect.github.com/theskumar/python-dotenv) | minor | `==1.1.1` → `==1.2.2` |

---

### Release Notes

<details>
<summary>theskumar/python-dotenv (python-dotenv)</summary>

### [`v1.2.2`](https://redirect.github.com/theskumar/python-dotenv/blob/HEAD/CHANGELOG.md#122---2026-03-01)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/theskumar/python-dotenv/compare/v1.2.1...v1.2.2)

##### Added

- Support for Python 3.14, including the free-threaded (3.14t) build. ([#​588](https://redirect.github.com/theskumar/python-dotenv/issues/588))

##### Changed

- The `dotenv run` command now forwards flags directly to the specified command by \[[@​bbc2](https://redirect.github.com/bbc2)] in \[[#​607](https://redirect.github.com/theskumar/python-dotenv/issues/607)]
- Improved documentation clarity regarding override behavior and the reference page.
- Updated PyPy support to version 3.11.
- Documentation for FIFO file support.
- Dropped Support for Python 3.9.

##### Fixed

- Improved `set_key` and `unset_key` behavior when interacting with symlinks by \[[@​bbc2](https://redirect.github.com/bbc2)] in \[[`790c5c0`](https://redirect.github.com/theskumar/python-dotenv/commit/790c5c0)]
- Corrected the license specifier and added missing Python 3.14 classifiers in package metadata by \[[@​JYOuyang](https://redirect.github.com/JYOuyang)] in \[[#​590](https://redirect.github.com/theskumar/python-dotenv/issues/590)]

##### Breaking Changes

- `dotenv.set_key` and `dotenv.unset_key` used to follow symlinks in some
  situations. This is no longer the case. For that behavior to be restored in
  all cases, `follow_symlinks=True` should be used.

- In the CLI, `set` and `unset` used to follow symlinks in some situations. This
  is no longer the case.

- `dotenv.set_key`, `dotenv.unset_key` and the CLI commands `set` and `unset`
  used to reset the file mode of the modified .env file to `0o600` in some
  situations. This is no longer the case: The original mode of the file is now
  preserved. Is the file needed to be created or wasn't a regular file, mode
  `0o600` is used.

### [`v1.2.1`](https://redirect.github.com/theskumar/python-dotenv/blob/HEAD/CHANGELOG.md#121---2025-10-26)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/theskumar/python-dotenv/compare/v1.2.0...v1.2.1)

- Move more config to `pyproject.toml`, removed `setup.cfg`
- Add support for reading `.env` from FIFOs (Unix) by \[[@​sidharth-sudhir](https://redirect.github.com/sidharth-sudhir)] in \[[#​586](https://redirect.github.com/theskumar/python-dotenv/issues/586)]

### [`v1.2.0`](https://redirect.github.com/theskumar/python-dotenv/blob/HEAD/CHANGELOG.md#120---2025-10-26)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/theskumar/python-dotenv/compare/v1.1.1...v1.2.0)

- Upgrade build system to use PEP 517 & PEP 518 to use `build` and `pyproject.toml` by \[[@​EpicWink](https://redirect.github.com/EpicWink)] in \[[#​583](https://redirect.github.com/theskumar/python-dotenv/issues/583)]
- Add support for Python 3.14 by \[[@​23f3001135](https://redirect.github.com/23f3001135)] in \[[#​579](https://redirect.github.com/theskumar/python-dotenv/issues/579)]
- Add support for disabling of `load_dotenv()` using `PYTHON_DOTENV_DISABLED` env var. by \[[@​matthewfranglen](https://redirect.github.com/matthewfranglen)] in \[[#​569](https://redirect.github.com/theskumar/python-dotenv/issues/569)]

</details>

---

### Configuration

items.body:
Bumps [requests](https://github.com/psf/requests) from 2.32.4 to 2.33.0.
Release notes

Sourced from requests's releases.

v2.33.0

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • 📣 Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. 📣

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

Deprecations

  • Dropped support for Python 3.9 following its end of support. (#7196)

Documentation

  • Various typo fixes and doc improvements.

New Contributors

  • @​M0d3v1 made their first contribution in psf/requests#6865
  • @​aminvakil made their first contribution in psf/requests#7220

items.title: Add IoT sensor failure prediction ML system
items.body:
Manufacturing equipment telemetry (temperature, vibration, pressure, RPM, voltage, error codes) requires a dedicated ML pipeline to predict machine failures before they occur. This adds a self-contained `src/iot/` module alongside the existing NYC Taxi pipeline.

## New module: `src/iot/`

- **`sensor_data.py`** — validates raw telemetry: out-of-bounds clamp → NaN, median imputation for sensor drift/gaps, UTC timestamp coercion, `add_failure_label()` that looks ahead `horizon_minutes` per machine, synthetic data generator for tests/demos
- **`features.py`** — per-machine rolling feature engineering over 10-min / 30-min / 1-h windows:
  - rolling mean, std, min, max, variance
  - OLS gradient (trend) on 10-min window
  - spike indicator: `value > μ + 3σ` within window
  - cyclic hour encoding (sin/cos), error code lag + rolling count
- **`train.py`** — trains Random Forest or Gradient Boosting with MLflow logging, saves `.pkl` + feature schema JSON; ships a CLI entry point

```bash
# Train on real data
python -m src.iot.train --data sensors.csv --algo rf

# Or use built-in synthetic data
python -m src.iot.train --algo gb
```

## API: `/predict/sensor-failure`

New POST endpoint on `api/main.py` accepting a batch of `SensorReading` objects. Returns `failure_probability` + `will_fail` flag per reading, with a heuristic fallback (error-code-based score) when no trained model is present. Prometheus metrics wired in.

```json
POST /predict/sensor-failure
{"readings": [{"timestamp": "2026-03-12T11:05:21Z", "machine_id": "machine_27",
               "temperature": 84.3, "vibration": 0.02, "pressure": 101.3,
               "rpm": 3500, "voltage": 220, "error_code": 0}]}
```

items.title: fix(lab-00): workflow correctness, shared EVE-NG helper, .unl shipping standard, docs
items.body:
## Summary

Four-commit PR hardening the switching lab-00 workflow end-to-end and
establishing repo-wide standards that future labs will inherit.

### 1. `6450c1a` fix(lab-00): workflow correctness, setup_lab robustness, VPC configs
- `workbook.md` Section 9: reorder workflow so `apply_solution.py` resets before inject; clarify `setup_lab.py` vs `apply_solution.py` roles
- `workbook.md` Ticket 1: drop misleading "and/or SW3" wording -- `inject_scenario_01.py` only modifies SW1
- `setup_lab.py`: drop broken `--ssh` flag; use `save_config()` (handles "Destination filename?" prompt); fail fast on unset `--host`
- Add missing `initial-configs/PC{1,2}.vpc` (required by EVE-NG skill §4)
- `requirements.txt`: add `requests` (needed by the new REST discovery helper)

### 2. `d2fb8d0` refactor(lab-00): shared EVE-NG helper with REST port discovery
- New `labs/common/tools/eve_ng.py`: `require_host()`, `discover_ports()` (EVE-NG REST), `connect_node()`, typed `EveNgError`
- Rewrite `setup_lab.py`, `apply_solution.py`, `inject_scenario_0{1,2,3}.py` to discover ports at runtime instead of hardcoding 32768-32771
- **Add pre-flight checks to inject scripts** -- verify target is in expected solution state before injecting; `--skip-preflight` escape hatch
- Meaningful exit codes: 2=missing host, 3=API error, 4=preflight fail

### 3. `8082647` feat(lab-00): `topology/` folder + .unl shipping standard
- Move `topology.drawio` into `topology/` subfolder
- New `topology/README.md` with import steps, export workflow, manual-rebuild fallback
- Bump `.agent/skills` submodule to pick up new Section 7 requiring `.unl` shipping (skills PR: FonNkwenti/cisco-lab-skills-eve-ng#1)
- Update `meta.yaml` files block: diagram/eve_ng_lab/readme

### 4. `aa618b4` docs: top-level and lab-level READMEs + gitignore local settings
- Expand root `README.md` with full VMware -> EVE-NG -> scripts -> workbook walkthrough
- New `labs/switching/lab-00-vlans-and-trunking/README.md` with Stage 0-5 flow
- `.gitignore` + untrack `.claude/settings.local.json`

## Known follow-ups (out of this PR)

- [ ] Export the actual `.unl` from a running EVE-NG instance and add to `topology/` (requires hands-on with the EVE-NG host)
- [ ] Apply the same refactor pattern to `ospf/lab-00` and subsequent labs
- [ ] Consider Python packaging (`pip install -e .`) once >5 labs share `labs/common/tools/`

## Test plan

- [ ] `python -m py_compile` on all 6 rewritten scripts (verified locally)
- [ ] With a running EVE-NG lab: `python setup_lab.py --host <ip>` pushes initial configs to all 4 nodes
- [ ] `apply_solution.py` -> `inject_scenario_01.py` -> verify PC1 cannot ping PC2 -> fix per workbook -> ping succeeds
- [ ] Repeat for scenarios 02 and 03
- [ ] Confirm inject scripts refuse to run without prior `apply_solution.py` (preflight)
- [ ] Merge skills PR #1 first, then this PR, so the submodule bump is consistent

items.title: Add SSM dynamics (S4, S5, Mamba) and drop-in SSMDynamics for TD-MPC2
items.body:
### Motivation
- Provide Phase 2 structured state-space world models (S4, S5, Mamba) so the TD-MPC2 dynamics can be swapped from an MLP to an SSM for stability, long-range memory, and rollout-error evaluation. 
- Expose the SSM variants as a drop-in replacement so downstream planning and Phase 3 work (MPPI, SimNorm, SAM) can be applied without changing trainer logic.

### Description
- Added an `ssm/` package with `s5_layer.py`, `s4_layer.py`, `mamba_layer.py`, and `ssm_world_model.py` implementing diagonal/stable recurrences and a `SSMDynamics` wrapper that projects state to latent space and manages hidden state via `reset_hidden` and `forward(z, a)`.
- Extended `TDMPC2Model` (`tdmpc2/model.py`) to accept `dynamics_type` and `ssm_state_dim`, instantiate either `MLPDynamics` or `SSMDynamics`, and reset SSM hidden state at the start of rollouts.
- Added rollout-error diagnostics to the trainer (`tdmpc2/trainer.py`) with new config fields `rollout_error_horizon` and `rollout_error_every_steps` and a helper `_compute_rollout_error_metric` that logs `rollout_error/h{H}` into metrics.
- Extended the CLI and entrypoints by adding `--dynamics-type` and `--ssm-state-dim` to `tdmpc2/train_tdmpc2.py`, created `train_tdmpc2_s4.py`, `train_tdmpc2_s5.py`, and `train_tdmpc2_mamba.py`, and exposed `tdmpc-s4`, `tdmpc-s5`, and `tdmpc-mamba` commands in `main.py`.

### Testing
- Compiled the codebase with `python -m compileall main.py tdmpc2 ssm train_tdmpc2_s4.py train_tdmpc2_s5.py train_tdmpc2_mamba.py`, which succeeded without syntax errors.
- Attempted a small runtime smoke test that constructs `TDMPC2Model` for each dynamics type and runs `rollout`, but this execution failed due to the environment missing `torch` (`ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'torch'`), so runtime validation could not be completed here.
- No other automated tests were added; existing training entrypoints and metrics logging were exercised via static checks and imports.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_e_69d4d95f0354832e8b4fed5dcd5993c5)

items.title: fix(deps): loosen pyarrow bounds — Streamlit Cloud install was failing on cmake source build
items.body:
## Summary

Streamlit Cloud install was dying with:

```
ERROR: Failed building wheel for pyarrow
error: command 'cmake' failed: No such file or directory
```

The previous pin `pyarrow>=16.0.0,<20` capped pyarrow at v19. When the resolver couldn't find a manylinux wheel matching the active Python interpreter inside that range, pip fell back to a source build of pyarrow. That requires `cmake` (and a full C++ toolchain), which Streamlit Cloud runners do not have. The install died before the app could boot.

## Fix

Drop the upper bound on `pyarrow` (and `pandas`, `numpy`, `streamlit`, `plotly`, `watchdog` as defense-in-depth) so the resolver always lands on a version that ships a prebuilt manylinux wheel for whatever Python ends up running. Lower bounds are kept at the known-good API floors the cockpit was built against. The pinning policy is now documented at the top of `requirements.txt`.

`requirements.txt` is the only file changed.

## Validation

Clean install on Python 3.14 (the most aggressive case — no wheels for the old pin range here at all) now succeeds end-to-end with **no source build**:

```
pyarrow=23.0.1  pandas=3.0.2  numpy=2.4.4  streamlit=1.56.0
```

All checks pass on the freshly installed stack:

- ✅ All `cockpit_core.*` modules import cleanly
- ✅ `streamlit_app.theme.adapt_theme` imports cleanly
- ✅ Parquet round-trip works (`pd.to_parquet` → `pd.read_parquet`)
- ✅ Snapshot helpers work

On Streamlit Cloud's Python 3.11 the resolver will pick a slightly older but equally cmake-free wheel.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI passes the `Lint & import check (Python 3.11)` job (which already runs `pip install -r requirements.txt`)
- [ ] After merge, redeploy the Streamlit Cloud app — install should complete and the app should boot

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

items.title: P2: six-layer architecture refactor (big-bang)
items.body:
## Summary

Big-bang рефакторинг монолитных `scripts/wisper_launcher.py` + `scripts/merge_whisperx.py` + `scripts/parse_fvtt_chat.py` в шестислойную архитектуру из `ARCHITECTURE.md`:

- `domain/` — чистые dataclass'ы (`SpeechEvent | ChatEvent | GameEvent`), `Timeline`, `speaker_map`
- `sources/` — `Source` ABC + `FasterWhisperSource`, `WhisperXSource`, `FvttChatSource`
- `mergers/` — `Merger` ABC + `ScriptMerger` (склейка same-speaker, speech-before-chat tiebreaker)
- `renderers/` — `Renderer` ABC + `PlainTextRenderer`
- `core/` — `pipeline.run` / `run_batch`, `PipelineParams` (frozen dataclass), `discovery`, `gpu_check`, `speaker_map`, `fvtt_helpers`, `cache` (stub)
- `ui/` — `cli.py` (argparse) + `gui.py` (tkinter, порт из `wisper_launcher.gui_main`) + `__main__.py` для `python -m ui`
- `launcher/` — bootstrap и PyInstaller spec переведены на `python -m ui`

Legacy-скрипты удалены (−1380 строк). Runtime-ссылок на них нет.

## Что сделано по шагам

- **P2.1–2.2**: scaffold + `domain/`
- **P2.3**: `sources/` (FasterWhisper, WhisperX с `beam_size=10`, FvttChat)
- **P2.4**: `mergers/` (`ScriptMerger` с glue + tiebreaker)
- **P2.5**: `renderers/` (`PlainTextRenderer`)
- **P2.6**: `core/pipeline.run` + `discovery` + `gpu_check` + `cache` stub
- **P2.7**: `ui/cli.py` с документированным исключением `ui → sources` для `argparse choices=`
- **P2.8**: `launcher/bootstrap.py` + `build.spec` на `python -m ui`, все шесть пакетов бандлятся
- **P2.9**: 3-tier e2e gate — tier 1 структурный (CI), tier 2 семантический (token overlap ≥ 0.90), tier 3 strict (manual)
- **P2.10**: удаление legacy-скриптов, `core/fvtt_helpers.detect_fvtt_tz_offset` для разрыва `ui → sources`, ренейминг `_parse_fvtt_log` → `parse_fvtt_log` и т.д. (reviewer finding)
- **Fix**: `_token_overlap` в tier2 тесте считал intersection над set но делил на длины list — идентичные тексты давали 0.77 вместо 1.0. Перешёл на Counter-based multiset intersection.

## Test plan

- [x] Tier 1 — 84 pytest green в 0.21s (`pytest -m "not slow"`)
- [x] Tier 2 — 2 pytest green в 102s, overlap **1.000** (byte-exact reproduction baseline на синтетическом FLAC-фикстуре, faster-whisper large-v3-ru-podlodka, CPU/int8/beam=1)
- [x] `python -m ui --help` работает
- [x] Полный import chain (`core.pipeline`, `core.fvtt_helpers`, `ui.cli`, `sources`, `mergers`, `renderers`, `domain.timeline`) — OK
- [ ] **Manual tier 3 smoke test** на реальной сессии (`games/bogomols/*`) — отложен в follow-up, покрыт tier 2 на синтетике

## Известные follow-up (не блокеры)

- Документация (`README.md`, `README.ru.md`, `00_README.md`, `01_Как_пользоваться.md`, `scripts/00_README.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`) всё ещё содержит инструкции `python scripts/wisper_launcher.py ...` — требует отдельного `docs:` PR
- `resolve_speaker` edge case: `foo` vs `foo.json` коллизия в `domain/speaker_map.py`
- `load_speaker_map` warning logging
- Унификация registry contract: `sources` возвращает `type[Source]`, `mergers`/`renderers` возвращают instance
- ADR-009 для документированного исключения `ui/cli.py → sources` (`argparse choices=`)

## Breaking changes

- Entry point: `python scripts/wisper_launcher.py` → `python -m ui`
- `scripts/run_whisperx_gui.bat` обновлён автоматически
- PyInstaller build: теперь бандлит шесть пакетов (`ui`, `core`, `sources`, `mergers`, `renderers`, `domain`) вместо трёх старых скриптов
- Дропнутые CLI-флаги: `--vad_method`, `--hf_token`, `--diarize` (soft-drop с warning через `argparse.SUPPRESS`)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Sourced from requests's releases.

v2.33.0

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • 📣 Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. 📣

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

Deprecations

  • Dropped support for Python 3.9 following its end of support. (#7196)

Documentation

  • Various typo fixes and doc improvements.

items.title: Fix Gate.io WS ingestion, handle full snapshots, and wire Python SHM signals; testnet tuning
items.body:
### Motivation
- Gate.io futures WS parsing and snapshot handling were dropping all L2 levels (no ticks reached book) and Python-generated trade intents in SHM were never consumed by Rust, causing zero trades.  
- Testnet parameters and strategy gates were too strict for thin/testnet liquidity and prevented fills.  
- Several robustness and interoperability issues (DB role expectations, small compile/test breakages) needed remediation for reliable end-to-end behavior.

### Description
- Reworked Gate.io WS ingestion to parse level objects (`p`/`s`), subscribe to `futures.tickers` for fast BBO bootstrapping, and publish `SNAPSHOT_START`/`SNAPSHOT_END` sentinels so full snapshots are applied correctly (changes in `rust_engine/src/main.rs`).
- Updated the orderbook builder to clear books on `SNAPSHOT_START`, apply deltas, and only publish snapshots once both sides exist, and added `FlatOrderBook::clear()` to safely reset levels (`rust_engine/src/flat_book.rs`, `rust_engine/src/main.rs`).
- Opened and polled the Python SHM signal queue (`SignalQueueConsumer::open()`), converted `TradeIntent`s into `OrderCommand`s with pre-trade risk checks and position-slot management inside the execution router loop (`rust_engine/src/main.rs`, `rust_engine/src/signal_queue.rs`).
- Tuned strategy and runtime parameters for testnet: reduced VPIN bucket size, relaxed spread/depth gates, lowered IOC/GTC thresholds, and adjusted per-pair profiles (including disabling `post_only`) in `strategy_engine.rs` and `crypto_trading_bot/config/engine_config.toml`.
- Added a small DB bootstrap to create a compatibility `postgres` role and adjusted docker-compose healthcheck expectations via `crypto_trading_bot/init-db.sql` and `crypto_trading_bot/docker-compose.yml` references.
- Minor test/compile fixes (import `serde_json::json` in Binance test module) and many whitespace/style safe adjustments; no behavioral changes outside the fixes above (`rust_engine/src/binance_gateway.rs`).

### Testing
- Ran `cargo build` in `rust_engine` and the build finished successfully (development profile, warnings remained).  
- Ran `cargo test --no-run` in `rust_engine` and compilation for tests completed successfully.  
- Ran `cargo fmt` which failed due to many trailing-whitespace occurrences in `rust_engine/src/main.rs` (these were flagged by rustfmt and are pre-existing/large; formatting not applied in this PR).

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_e_69cb479347f08332b0c0371ac9fb7058)

items.title: fix: resolve Dependabot security alerts in dev-requirements.txt
items.body:
## Summary

Resolves all 43 open Dependabot security alerts by updating vulnerable dependency version constraints in \dev-requirements.txt\.

## Changes

### Pillow
- **Python <=3.7**: \6.2.0\ → \9.5.0\ (last version to support Python 3.7)
  - Fixes ~20 high/critical CVEs across Pillow 6.x–9.x
- **Python ==3.8**: loosened pin from \==10.3.0\ to \>=10.3.0\ (Pillow 11+ dropped Python 3.8; 10.3.0 remains the highest safe version)
  - No functional change, just ensures pip resolves to a non-vulnerable release
- **Python >3.8**: already at \12.1.1\ — no change needed

### pywin32 (alert #16)
- Python <=3.6 line: changed from \<=227\ (vulnerable) to \>=301\
- Python >3.6 line: bumped minimum from \>=300\ to \>=301\
- Fixes integer overflow CVE resolved in pywin32 301

## Testing
No code changes — only version constraints updated. CI (AppVeyor) will validate across all supported Python versions.

items.body:
Bumps [cairosvg](https://github.com/Kozea/CairoSVG) from 2.8.2 to 2.9.0.
Release notes

Sourced from cairosvg's releases.

2.9.0

WARNING: this is a security update.

Using a lot of recursively nested use tags could lead to long rendering times with relatively small inputs. CairoSVG now stops rendering when more than 100k use tags are rendered.

Using the --unsafe option allows to render larger documents.

  • Drop support of Python 3.9, add support of Python 3.14
Changelog

Sourced from cairosvg's changelog.

Version 2.9.0 released on 2026-03-13

WARNING: this is a security update.

Using a lot of recursively nested use tags could lead to long rendering times with relatively small inputs. CairoSVG now stops rendering when more than 100k use tags are rendered.

Using the --unsafe option allows to render larger documents.

  • Drop support of Python 3.9, add support of Python 3.14

Sourced from requests's releases.

v2.33.0

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • 📣 Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. 📣

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

Deprecations

  • Dropped support for Python 3.9 following its end of support. (#7196)

Documentation

  • Various typo fixes and doc improvements.

items.title: Add datasets, capstone projects, and docx generator for OI textbook
items.body:
Continuing textbook content creation (Steps 15–17): datasets documentation, five capstone projects, and a Markdown-to-Word compiler.

### Datasets
- `datasets/README.md` — Overview of five dataset categories (MEA electrophysiology, organoid imaging, simulation outputs, genomic/transcriptomic, benchmarks) with Python loading snippets and licensing
- `datasets/datasets_index.md` — 15 indexed datasets (DS-001–DS-015) with format, size, license, citation, related chapters, and access DOIs

### Capstone Projects
- **Capstone 1** — Hodgkin-Huxley neuron simulation: HH equation implementation, f-I curves, 10-neuron coupled network, cross-correlation analysis
- **Capstone 2** — Organoid fabrication plan: iPSC differentiation protocol, maturation timeline (Week 0–20), vascularization strategy, QC checkpoints, cost estimation
- **Capstone 3** — MEA signal processing pipeline: bandpass filtering, spike detection/sorting (PCA + k-means), burst detection, raster plots
- **Capstone 4** — Reservoir computing: ESN implementation, NARMA-10 benchmark, spectral radius sweep, biological reservoir with Dale's law and small-world topology
- **Capstone 5** — Integrated OI system design (final project): 5 application domains, 6-phase timeline, simulation component, ethics review, 200-point rubric

### Document Generator
- `generate_docx.py` — Compiles 24 chapters + 5 appendices into `.docx` using `python-docx`. Handles heading hierarchy, inline formatting, code blocks, tables, LaTeX→Unicode conversion, title page, and TOC placeholder.

```python
# Usage
pip install python-docx
python generate_docx.py
# → organoid_intelligence_textbook.docx
```

Bumps the pip group with 1 update in the / directory: [requests](https://github.com/psf/requests). Updates `requests` from 2.32.4 to 2.33.0
Release notes

Sourced from requests's releases.

v2.33.0

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • 📣 Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. 📣

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

Deprecations

  • Dropped support for Python 3.9 following its end of support. (#7196)

Documentation

  • Various typo fixes and doc improvements.

items.title: Switch Dockerfile to Python 3.13 via uv, drop /opt/venv
items.body:
Replaces the Ubuntu default Python + `/opt/venv` virtualenv setup with Python 3.13 installed and managed entirely by `uv`, with all dependencies landing in system site-packages.

**Python 3.13 installation — no PPA**
- Bootstrap `uv` using Ubuntu's built-in `python3-pip` (sole purpose)
- `uv python install 3.13` replaces deadsnakes PPA; symlink uv-managed binary to `/usr/local/bin/python3{,.13}`

**uv configuration — system install, no venv**
- Drop `UV_PROJECT_ENVIRONMENT=/opt/venv` and venv `PATH` prepend
- Add `UV_SYSTEM_PYTHON=1` + `UV_PYTHON=3.13`; all `uv sync` / `uv pip install` calls target Python 3.13 system site-packages
- Remove `--python /opt/venv/bin/python` from vLLM stable and nightly install commands

```dockerfile
# Before
ENV UV_PROJECT_ENVIRONMENT="/opt/venv" \
    PATH="/opt/venv/bin:${PATH}"
RUN uv pip install --python /opt/venv/bin/python "vllm[flashinfer]==0.17.0" ...

# After
RUN uv python install 3.13 && \
    ln -s "$(uv python find 3.13)" /usr/local/bin/python3.13 && \
    ln -sf /usr/local/bin/python3.13 /usr/local/bin/python3
ENV UV_SYSTEM_PYTHON=1 \
    UV_PYTHON=3.13
RUN uv pip install "vllm[flashinfer]==0.17.0" ...
```

`CMD` and model-download steps are unchanged; `python3` now resolves to 3.13 via the symlink.

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> Update the Dockerfile to switch the system Python to 3.13 and install all dependencies using uv into the system Python 3.13 environment (no /opt/venv virtualenv).
> 
> Context:
> - Repo: hotung1027/worker-vllm
> - File: Dockerfile
> - Current Dockerfile uses Ubuntu 22.04 base `nvidia/cuda:12.9.1-devel-ubuntu22.04`, installs `python3-pip`, sets `UV_PROJECT_ENVIRONMENT=/opt/venv`, runs `uv sync --no-dev`, and installs vLLM with `uv pip install --python /opt/venv/bin/python ...`.
> 
> Required changes:
> 1) Install and use **Python 3.13** as the system `python3` in the image.
>    - Ensure `python3` resolves to Python 3.13 (e.g., via apt packages + update-alternatives or explicit symlinks).
>    - Ensure `pip`/`pip3` work for Python 3.13.
> 2) Change the build to use **uv** to install dependencies into the **system Python 3.13** environment (not a virtualenv).
>    - Remove `UV_PROJECT_ENVIRONMENT=/opt/venv` and related PATH modifications.
>    - Update `uv sync --no-dev` invocation to target system python (set `UV_PYTHON` or use `uv ... --python /usr/bin/python3.13` or appropriate mechanism so installs land in the system environment).
>    - Update vLLM install commands to use system Python 3.13 (remove `--python /opt/venv/bin/python`, replace with system Python 3.13).
> 3) Keep existing behavior of caching where possible (`--mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/uv`).
> 4) Keep existing model-download logic and entrypoint, but make sure they run under Python 3.13.
> 5) Make sure image still builds: include any required apt dependencies for building/installing Python 3.13 and wheels, and clean apt lists where sensible.
> 
> Notes/constraints:
> - Maintain CUDA compatibility steps already present (ldconfig etc.).
> - Use minimal changes outside Python/uv install behavior.
> - Ensure the resulting Dockerfile uses python3.13 explicitly where needed and `CMD` uses the correct python.
> 
> 
> The following is the prior conversation context from the user's chat exploration (may be truncated):
> 
> User: change system python to 3.13,
> and use uv to install the dependecies to system python3.13
> 
> Assistant: To do that in `hotung1027/worker-vllm/Dockerfile`, you’ll want to:
> 
> 1) **Install system Python 3.13** (instead of using Ubuntu’s default `python3`), and make `python3` point to 3.13.
> 2) **Stop using `/opt/venv`** and instead tell `uv` to install into **system Python 3.13’s site-packages** (i.e., “system install”).
> 3) Update all `python3 ...` and `uv pip ... --python ...` calls to target **`/usr/bin/python3.13`** (or just `python3` after alternatives are set).
> 
> Before I change the file, one key clarification because it affects the exact Dockerfile edits:
> 
> - Do you want **true system installs** (packages go into the image’s global site-packages for Python 3.13), or do you still want an **isolated environment** but using Python 3.13 (i.e., a venv at `/opt/venv` built from Python 3.13)?
>   - You said “use uv to install the dependencies to system python3.13” → that sounds like **true system installs** (no venv). Confirm?
> 
> If you confirm “no venv, install into system Python 3.13”, tell me whether you want me to:
> - **(A)** just provide the patched Dockerfile content here, or
> - **(B)** open a PR in `hotung1027/worker-vllm` with the Dockerfile change (you’d need to explicitly ask “open a PR” for me to do that).
> 
> User: open a PRｔｏ　ｄｏ　ｔｈａｔ

items.title: feat: parallelize per-repo parse_repo in analyze command
items.body:
## Summary

`analyze` コマンドでリポジトリ単位の `SourceParser.parse_repo()` を並列実行できるようにする。複数リポジトリ構成（例: backend + frontend + worker）で解析時間をリポ数に対してほぼリニアに短縮。

### 主要な変更

- `ThreadPoolExecutor` ベースの並列解析を追加（LLM 呼び出しは I/O バウンドなので threading で十分な効果）
- 新しい CLI オプション `--parallel/-j N`（デフォルト `0` = 自動: `min(4, pending_repo数)`）
- `all` コマンドにも `--parallel` を伝播
- 失敗したリポジトリは `completed_repos` に入れず、`--resume` で再試行可能
- チェックポイント schema は無変更（既存の `_checkpoint.json` はそのまま読める）

### アーキテクチャ

4 つの小さな helper に分解してテスタビリティを確保:

1. `_resolve_parallel(parallel, repo_count) -> int` — CLI 値を max_workers に解決、負数は `typer.BadParameter`
2. `_parse_single_repo(repo_path, parser) -> RepoParseResult` — 例外を catch して uniform な結果を返す worker
3. `_run_parallel_parse(pending_repos, parser, parallel, on_complete) -> (successes, failures)` — `ThreadPoolExecutor` + `as_completed` + コールバック
4. `RepoParseResult` dataclass — 成功・失敗を同型で扱うコンテナ（`analyzer/source_parser.py`）

共有状態の更新は `on_complete` コールバック経由でメインスレッドに集約してロックフリー。`all_contexts` は `ThreadPoolExecutor` の外で `config.repo_paths` 順に prebuild することで順序を保証。

### 参考ドキュメント

- Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-11-analyze-parallel-design.md`
- Plan: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-11-analyze-parallel.md`

## Test plan

- [x] `tests/test_analyze_parallel.py` 新規追加（18 テスト: `RepoParseResult` 3 / `_resolve_parallel` 7 / `_parse_single_repo` 3 / `_run_parallel_parse` 5）
- [x] 既存テスト 13 件含め、計 31 テスト pass
- [x] `python main.py analyze --help` で `--parallel/-j` オプション表示確認
- [x] `python main.py all --help` で `--parallel/-j` オプション表示確認
- [x] 3 リポ `--parallel 2 --skip-llm` で並列実行（完了順が投入順と異なることを確認）
- [x] チェックポイント書き込み・`--resume` でスキップ動作確認
- [x] `--parallel -1` で `typer.BadParameter` + exit 2 を確認
- [ ] 実リポジトリ + 実 LLM での smoke test（レビュー後）

items.title: Fix GA8 atlas packing and CELM envelope for macOS 13+
items.body:
Two bugs caused template (grayscale+alpha) images packed into atlases to
render incorrectly on macOS 14, manifesting as missing or garbled icons
in apps like Rectangle

Packer: GA8 sub-image positioning was using a fixed 4-bytes-per-pixel
stride, but vImage's deepmap2 encoder reads GA8 data at 2 bpp.
Sub-images placed at byte offset `x * 4` past the `width * 2` boundary
fell into the row padding region that vImage skipped, so most of the GA8
atlas content was never encoded.  Use the actual pixel size (2 for GA8,
4 for BGRA) for both the buffer stride and sub-image positioning

Compression envelope: Apple's actool emits CELM ver=0 (with the 16-byte
sub-header) for DMP2-compressed packed atlases when min_deploy is
macOS 13+.  We were unconditionally emitting ver=2.  CoreUI on macOS
14 decodes ver=2 GA8 atlases as kCGImageAlphaNoneSkipLast, dropping
the alpha channel entirely.  Switch to ver=0 for macOS >= 13.0; older
targets still use ver=2 to match Apple's behaviour there
