items.title: pg_health: PG18 compatibility fixes and container setup
items.body:
## Summary

- Fix `pg_stat_user_tables` column renames in PG18 (`tablename` → `relname`) across `BLOAT_SQL` and `VACUUM_SQL`
- Fix ambiguous `relname` in `VACUUM_SQL` JOIN; switch to `relid = pg_class.oid`
- Drop `readonly=True` from psycopg2 session — PG18 blocks stats/catalog views in readonly transactions
- Replace fragile `pg_available_extensions` check in `slow-queries` with a direct try/except
- Add `shared_preload_libraries=pg_stat_statements` to the local postgres container
- Publish container on port `5433` to avoid conflict with a local PG instance on 5432
- README: venv setup steps, local container env vars (with correct port), `pg_stat_statements` first-launch instructions

## Test plan

- [ ] `python pg_health/pg_health.py bloat` returns results without error
- [ ] `python pg_health/pg_health.py vacuum` returns results without error
- [ ] `python pg_health/pg_health.py slow-queries` works after creating the extension
- [ ] `python pg_health/pg_health.py connections` and `index-usage` still clean
- [ ] Container starts on port 5433 with `docker compose --profile local up -d`

items.title: Add retro CCD camera filter pipeline (halation, crushed blacks, film grain, split-tone)
items.body:
Implements a modular Python/OpenCV filter that transforms modern, clinical-looking digital footage into the characteristic aesthetic of a late-2000s CCD camera (Fujifilm/Canon PowerShot era).

## Pipeline stages (`filter.py`)

- **`apply_halation`** — screen-blends a heavily blurred highlight mask (luma > threshold) back over the image, simulating CCD charge bleed around light sources
- **`crush_blacks`** — applies a power-curve LUT below a configurable knee, pulling shadows toward pure black instead of lifting them
- **`apply_color_shift`** — luminance-weighted additive split-tone: warm cream push at mid-tones (~0.45 luma), cool teal push in shadows (< 0.35 luma)
- **`add_film_grain`** — generates Gaussian noise at reduced resolution then upscales it, approximating silver-halide crystal clustering rather than uniform sensor noise
- **`apply_ccd_filter`** — composes all four stages in order: halation → crush → color → grain

## CLI (`demo.py`)

Supports both image and video input with per-parameter tuning:

```bash
python demo.py input.jpg output.jpg --halation 0.4 --crush 0.2 --grain 0.05 --warmth 0.08 --teal 0.06
python demo.py input.mp4 output.mp4
```

items.title: feat: add `rrt ci-version` command for mixed Python + Cargo CI versioning
items.body:
Repos with mixed Python + Rust metadata needed a custom CI versioning script (`scripts/ci_version.py`) even after adopting `repo-release-tools`, because `rrt bump` only handles release versions — not the per-run dev pre-releases required for TestPyPI uploads. This adds `rrt ci-version` to close that gap, driven by `[tool.rrt]` config rather than hard-coded paths.

## Config

New optional `ci_format` field on `[[tool.rrt.version_targets]]`:

```toml
[[tool.rrt.version_targets]]
path = "pyproject.toml"
kind = "pep621"
ci_format = "pep440"        # → 0.2.0.dev12345601

[[tool.rrt.version_targets]]
path = "Cargo.toml"
section = "package"
field = "version"
ci_format = "semver_pre"    # → 0.2.0-dev.12345601
```

Targets without `ci_format` are untouched by `ci-version` (only affected by `bump`).

## New command: `rrt ci-version`

Three subcommands mirroring the original script's interface:

- **`compute`** — prints the published version for the current run to stdout (shell-capturable). Reads `GITHUB_REF`, `GITHUB_RUN_ID`, `GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT`; all overridable via flags.
  - Tag build (`refs/tags/v*`) → tag version with `v` stripped
  - `refs/heads/main` → `{base}.dev{GITHUB_RUN_ID}{GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT:02d}` (PEP 440)
  - Any other ref → base version unchanged
- **`apply VERSION`** — applies an explicit version string to all `ci_format`-configured targets. `pep440` targets receive it unchanged; `semver_pre` targets receive it after converting `.dev{N}` → `-dev.{N}`.
- **`sync`** — `compute` + `apply` in one step; direct replacement for `ci_version.py sync`.

```bash
# Drop-in replacement for: python scripts/ci_version.py sync
rrt ci-version sync

# Or capture and apply separately (e.g. to set a GitHub Actions output):
VERSION=$(rrt ci-version compute)
echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
rrt ci-version apply "$VERSION"
```

## Changes

- **`config.py`** — `VALID_CI_FORMATS` constant; `ci_format` field on `VersionTarget`; validation added to `validate()`.
- **`commands/ci_version.py`** *(new)* — `GitHubContext`, `to_semver()`, `compute_published_version()`, and the three command handlers.
- **`cli.py`** — registers `ci-version`.

items.title: Easy transfer-prep + Next.js demo scaffold for easy-sandbox
items.body:
## Summary

Prepares FagSmart for transfer to the Easy organisation (**Vei 2**) and
adds a ready-to-port Next.js demo scaffold for `easy-sandbox` (**Vei 3**).

### Vei 2 — Easy-ready standalone repo

- **Databricks auth**: support service-principal OAuth M2M
  (`DATABRICKS_CLIENT_ID` + `DATABRICKS_CLIENT_SECRET`) in addition to
  PAT; `DatabricksClient` auto-selects, raises on neither set
- **easy-api-core client**: new `api_clients/easy_api_core.py` with
  typed wrappers for eiendom / address / buildings, uses
  `EASY_API_CORE_URL` + `EASY_API_CORE_KEY`
- **Production Dockerfile**: multi-stage, non-root, healthchecked with
  `fagsmart-lint`, no runtime network needed
- **GitHub Actions CI**: pytest + `fagsmart-lint --json` + ruff + docker
  build smoke test
- **Ruff clean**: fixed E741, F841, N801, E402; added sensible
  per-file-ignores for existing SQL/MLflow/test long lines
- **TRANSFER.md**: handoff plan for the platform team — why FagSmart
  can't live in `easy-sandbox` (multi-service + stateful), infra
  recommendations (Neo4j AuraDB, Azure PG Flexible Server + pgvector,
  Azure OpenAI), mirror steps, env var checklist
- **README.md**: rewritten to reflect v2 state (4 real agents, 5 typed
  stubs, 86 passing tests, confidence + contradictions, lint CLI)

### Vei 3 — Demo companion for easy-sandbox

- `easy-sandbox-app/` scaffold ready to drop into
  `easy-sandbox/apps/fagsmart-demo/`
- Single-page Next.js 15 app (Tailwind, React 18, Zod)
- TypeScript port of the søknadsplikt decision tree
  (`lib/classify.ts` + `lib/rules-data.ts`) — mirrors the Python
  `RulesEngine` for the `bokstav a` tree + SAK10 § 4-1 exemption
- Confidence + boundary + contradiction warnings match the Python
  enrichment logic
- Optional `FAGSMART_BACKEND_URL` env lets the classify API route
  proxy to the full Python backend without UI changes
- Uses `EASY_API_CORE_URL` / `EASY_API_CORE_KEY` server-side for
  property lookups
- `easy-sandbox-app/README.md` has the copy-paste steps to port

### Testing

```
86 passing · 4 s · ruff clean
  └─ +6 easy-api-core tests (config validation + X-API-KEY header)
```

## Test plan

- [ ] `pip install -e ".[dev]"` installs cleanly
- [ ] `pytest` → 86 passed
- [ ] `ruff check src tests` → clean
- [ ] `fagsmart-lint` → 0 errors
- [ ] `docker build -t fagsmart .` succeeds; `docker run --rm fagsmart`
      reports `fagsmart-lint: N files checked … all good.`
- [ ] Inside `easy-sandbox-app/`: `npm install && npm run dev` serves a
      working classifier at `http://localhost:3000`
- [ ] Inside `easy-sandbox-app/`: `npm run build` succeeds; typecheck
      passes
- [ ] Hand off `TRANSFER.md` to the platform team

items.title: feat(day3): Lua templates + safe Docker sandbox execution
items.body:
## Что сделано

Day 3: реализован безопасный pipeline исполнения валидного YAML в Lua.

### 1) Core runtime
- строгий template selector operation -> template (без fallback)
- Lua codegen через шаблоны
- AST -> to_lua для array_filter condition
- luac -p проверка внутри Docker
- Docker sandbox executor (timeout=5s, memory=128MB, network=none)

### 2) API + модели + тесты
- добавлен POST /execute (session_id или inline yaml)
- добавлен контракт результата исполнения: status, stdout, stderr, exit_code
- /health расширен признаком доступности Docker
- добавлены Day 3 тесты

### 3) Документация
- README обновлён под /execute и sandbox contract
- docs/days/day3.md дополнен фактом реализации и артефактами

## Инкрементальные коммиты
- 42fcc19 feat(day3-core): add Lua runtime layer (selector, codegen, luac validator, docker sandbox)
- 5bde633 feat(day3-api): add /execute endpoint, execution contract models, and day3 tests
- 71045ca docs(day3): document /execute flow, sandbox contract, and delivered day3 artifacts

## Проверка
- pytest tests/ -q
- результат: 13 passed

  • #23350 Fix reference counting errors in registerNewType.
  • #23399, #23436, #23138 Fixed ChAruco and diamond boards detector bindings.
  • #23371 Added bindings to allow GpuMat and Stream objects to be initialized from memory initialized in other libraries

items.title: Refactor to 1.0.0: fix all broken converters, improve test coverage, clean up design
items.body:
- Fix all convert_* method signatures for markdownify >=1.0 (parent_tags replaces convert_as_inline)
- Rename convert_hn -> convert_hN to match markdownify 1.x dispatch
- Replace MrkdwnOptions class-as-namespace with MrkdwnConverter.Options inner class; drop _todict private API
- Add 'p' to convert list so multi-paragraph content is properly separated
- Fix OL numbering: use find_previous_siblings('li') instead of parent.index(el) which counted whitespace nodes
- Fix checkbox detection: use el.find('input') + has_attr('checked') to handle boolean attribute and whitespace leading children
- Restore nested list indentation using re_line_with_content from markdownify
- Fix angle bracket escaping: override escape() to encode literal < and > for Slack safety
- Remove render_tables option (produced unusable Markdown table syntax in Slack)
- Remove tabulate_newlines (invisible difference in Slack, fragile implementation)
- Delete consecutive-headers and highlighted-code-block fixtures (coincidental/cut behavior)
- Rewrite test suite: parametrized per-fixture tests + targeted unit tests for each bug
- Add .github/workflows/test.yml: pytest on Python 3.9-3.12 matrix on every push/PR
- Pin markdownify to >=1.0,<2; update Python support to 3.9+
- Bump version to 1.0.0

items.body:

items.title: Bound inter-process data queue and tighten exception handling
items.body:
`mp.Queue()` was unbounded, allowing the producer (500 Hz control loop) to grow memory without limit when the UI consumer falls behind. The bare `except:` in the `put` path was silently swallowing all exceptions, including serialization errors.

## Changes

- **`main.py`**: Replace `mp.Queue()` with `mp.Queue(maxsize=100)` — caps buffering to ~0.2 s at 500 Hz; excess packets are dropped by the producer via `block=False`
- **`main.py`**: Remove unused `from typing import Any` import
- **`core/engine.py`**: Narrow bare `except:` to `except queue.Full:` so only the expected drop-when-full case is silent; other errors (e.g. pickling failures) now propagate

```python
# Before
data_queue = mp.Queue()
...
except:
    pass

# After
data_queue = mp.Queue(maxsize=100)
...
except queue.Full:
    pass
```

items.title: [Aikido] Fix security issue in python-multipart via minor version upgrade from 0.0.6 to 0.0.18
items.body:
Upgrade python-multipart to fix ReDoS vulnerability in Content-Type parsing and DoS from malformed multipart boundaries.
⚠️ Breaking changes in this upgrade
All breaking changes by upgrading python-multipart from version 0.0.6 to 0.0.18 ([CHANGELOG](https://github.com/Kludex/python-multipart/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md)) | Version | Description | | --- | --- | | 
0.0.9
| Drop support for Python 3.7 | | 
0.0.13
| Rename import to `python_multipart` | | 
0.0.18
| Hard break if found data after last boundary on `MultipartParser` |
✅ 2 CVEs resolved by this upgrade
This PR will resolve the following CVEs: | Issue | Severity           | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | 
[CVE-2024-24762](https://app.aikido.dev/issues/25487533/detail?groupId=79519#CVE-2024-24762)
| 
HIGH
| [python-multipart] A ReDoS vulnerability in HTTP Content-Type header parsing allows attackers to send malicious form data that causes excessive CPU consumption and indefinite stalling, preventing the server from handling additional requests. | | 
[AIKIDO-2024-10493](https://app.aikido.dev/issues/25487533/detail?groupId=79519#AIKIDO-2024-10493)
| 
LOW
| [python-multipart] A maliciously crafted multipart request with junk data after the boundary causes resource exhaustion, leading to Denial of Service. The server becomes unresponsive and unable to process further requests. |

items.title: Implement Rust BMAP library and bmapctl CLI
items.body:
## Summary

- Full Rust implementation of the BMAP protocol library (`bmap` crate)
- Same layered architecture as Python: protocol → transport → connection → CLI
- `bmapctl` binary — single 599KB native executable
- `Transport` trait enables mock testing without Bluetooth hardware
- QC Ultra 2 fully supported, QC35 stubbed
- Device configs are data structs with `Option<Addr>` fields (compile-time feature checking)

## Verified against real hardware

```
$ cargo run -- status
  Battery      90%
  Mode         immersion
  CNC          ░░░░░░░░░░ 0/10
  EQ           +0/+0/+0 (bass/mid/treble)
  Name         Fargo
  FW           8.2.20+g34cf029
  Sidetone     medium
  Multipoint   on
  AutoPause    on
  Prompts      off (US English)
```

## Test plan

- [x] `make rust-test` — 41 tests pass (protocol, parsers, devices, connection with mock)
- [x] `make python-test` — 91 tests still pass
- [x] `cargo run -- status/battery/buttons/eq` — verified against QC Ultra 2
- [x] `cargo build --release` — 599KB static binary

items.title: Diuguid patch 040826
items.body: re worked UI screen. There will likely be merge conflicts since this was built from the boiler plate commit. apologies, I had to start from there. I added a column, category. I think we can use this in the analytics section. Below are the files that I have tested with, the process will accept anything with the prefix: "cu.data": https://download.bls.gov/pub/time.series/cu/cu.data.15.USMedical https://download.bls.gov/pub/time.series/cu/cu.data.12.USHousing user experience: 1. docker compose down -v, docker compose up -d, navigate to http://localhost:8501/ for the UI and http://localhost:8080 to open Kafka 2. choose a file to upload. will accept anything with prefix: "cu.data" 3. add a category name 4. click load data 5. manually start consumer - **docker compose exec app python -m etl.consumer** 6. monitor the streamlit logs - **docker compose logs streamlit -f** - when you hit load data, the producer is triggered and logs to the above command. manually start the consumer in another terminal - you will get a success message in the UI once the load is complete. - database should hold the records now.

Bumps [requests](https://github.com/psf/requests) from 2.32.5 to 2.33.0.
Release notes

Sourced from requests's releases.

v2.33.0

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • 📣 Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. 📣

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

Deprecations

  • Dropped support for Python 3.9 following its end of support. (#7196)

Documentation

  • Various typo fixes and doc improvements.

items.title: Development
items.body:
This pull request introduces significant improvements to the `yamlsub` project, focusing on modernizing the build and packaging process, enhancing documentation, updating Python compatibility, and improving code quality and testing. The most important changes are grouped and summarized below.

**Build & Packaging Modernization:**

- Migrated from `setup.cfg` to a modern `pyproject.toml`-based build system, specifying project metadata, dependencies, dev tools, and configuration for tools like Ruff and pytest. This also includes a new versioning scheme and automation with bumpver. (`[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-50c86b7ed8ac2cf95bd48334961bf0530cdc77b5a56f852c5c61b89d735fd711R1-R114)`, `[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-50c86b7ed8ac2cf95bd48334961bf0530cdc77b5a56f852c5c61b89d735fd711L17-R129)`, `[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-fa602a8a75dc9dcc92261bac5f533c2a85e34fcceaff63b3a3a81d9acde2fc52L1-L36)`)
- Updated the Python version requirement to 3.12 throughout the project, including `.python-version` and dependency specifications. (`[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-652bad7c1002badcf13f8de3a5096c2724ce54ae36cd68a21ad280547dff8e87R1)`, `[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-50c86b7ed8ac2cf95bd48334961bf0530cdc77b5a56f852c5c61b89d735fd711R1-R114)`)
- Replaced the manual and outdated GitHub Actions workflows with modern, version-pinned workflows for documentation build/deploy (`docs.yml`) and PyPI publishing (`python-publish.yml`), using `uv` for dependency management and builds. (`[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-9cf2000c53760d837a449f874e53f792819108d3a4bf346336d0f7d082deae2cR1-R52)`, `[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-87c8be2ac3b248aef84cc474af838d7cc461b7f8fb7f5444ac75f4d8fc02e377L1-R1)`, `[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-87c8be2ac3b248aef84cc474af838d7cc461b7f8fb7f5444ac75f4d8fc02e377L25-L39)`, `[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-546f37fac6467ab26e4179223b2906255b2376dcec04d05896317574b684a6d6L1-L32)`)

**Documentation Enhancements:**

- Added a full Sphinx documentation setup in the `docs/` directory, including configuration, Makefile, and API reference generation. The documentation now covers installation, usage, and API details, and is set up for GitHub Pages deployment. (`[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-85933aa74a2d66c3e4dcdf7a9ad8397f5a7971080d34ef1108296a7c6b69e7e3R1-R29)`, `[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-cdec797dddf8fc387304501d6bd4f318e1e9067c6fae012dfa8b69fd85fd3248R1-R12)`, `[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-8d068e8797e88947c320f79e856c3e16a72b730124a8f9d7031e2c4680dfa534R1-R18)`, `[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-311fffb2f63c2d97ead8bee2b1b6ea35591871552bc00d572c3670212b043a34R1-R63)`, `[[5]](diffhunk://#diff-0ee295c29def16f570f86378eff8ba37315e464d415e731d5d8ea2e67ce16f68R1-R7)`)
- Improved and clarified the `README.md` with updated project description, installation, and usage instructions, including examples for both pip and uv. (`[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5L1-R3)`, `[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5L18-R85)`)

**Codebase Improvements:**

- Refactored the main `Config` class for clarity, improved docstrings, and updated parameter names and types. Copyright/license headers were added to source files. (`[src/yamlsub/config.pyR1-R48](diffhunk://#diff-3c57cd8e9df0a607a7173bc8e5750c35f79ef459db3e5e160e3f7f55bab66c64R1-R48)`)
- Added an explicit `__version__` and `__all__` to the package’s `__init__.py`, and included license/copyright. (`[src/yamlsub/__init__.pyR1-R19](diffhunk://#diff-f0a668595de24df1bb33024f64aec3b8821759b5d6e5e6cc963bfe1106358ad2R1-R19)`)

**Testing Improvements:**

- Replaced the legacy unittest-based test with a modern pytest-style test in `tests/test_config.py`, improving readability and maintainability. Updated test resource naming for clarity. (`[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-da24b355349b53e4c8047034767d008e4c2ce9ea6c8933a3acd50d0828f00528R1-R35)`, `[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-dfab66f7ddfc66e42047e67be728008d669514617f6cb04e78d01f738d338ed5R1)`, `[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-1b6a15ac176adc798a25fe502aaaaa2b4bac655ecd2bb4760d8ec20478a07eceL1-L21)`)

**Cleanup:**

- Removed obsolete files such as `setup.cfg` and the old manual workflow. (`[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-fa602a8a75dc9dcc92261bac5f533c2a85e34fcceaff63b3a3a81d9acde2fc52L1-L36)`, `[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-546f37fac6467ab26e4179223b2906255b2376dcec04d05896317574b684a6d6L1-L32)`)

These changes collectively modernize the project, streamline development and deployment, and provide a better experience for both users and contributors.

- Build & Packaging:
  - Migrated to `pyproject.toml` for build and dependency management, dropped `setup.cfg`, and fully specified project metadata and tool configs. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-50c86b7ed8ac2cf95bd48334961bf0530cdc77b5a56f852c5c61b89d735fd711R1-R114) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-50c86b7ed8ac2cf95bd48334961bf0530cdc77b5a56f852c5c61b89d735fd711L17-R129) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-fa602a8a75dc9dcc92261bac5f533c2a85e34fcceaff63b3a3a81d9acde2fc52L1-L36)
  - Updated GitHub Actions workflows for documentation and publishing, using `uv` and version-pinned actions. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-9cf2000c53760d837a449f874e53f792819108d3a4bf346336d0f7d082deae2cR1-R52) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-87c8be2ac3b248aef84cc474af838d7cc461b7f8fb7f5444ac75f4d8fc02e377L1-R1) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-87c8be2ac3b248aef84cc474af838d7cc461b7f8fb7f5444ac75f4d8fc02e377L25-L39)
  - Set Python version to 3.12 project-wide. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-652bad7c1002badcf13f8de3a5096c2724ce54ae36cd68a21ad280547dff8e87R1) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-50c86b7ed8ac2cf95bd48334961bf0530cdc77b5a56f852c5c61b89d735fd711R1-R114)

- Documentation:
  - Added Sphinx documentation with API reference, usage examples, and GitHub Pages deployment. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-85933aa74a2d66c3e4dcdf7a9ad8397f5a7971080d34ef1108296a7c6b69e7e3R1-R29) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-cdec797dddf8fc387304501d6bd4f318e1e9067c6fae012dfa8b69fd85fd3248R1-R12) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-8d068e8797e88947c320f79e856c3e16a72b730124a8f9d7031e2c4680dfa534R1-R18) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-311fffb2f63c2d97ead8bee2b1b6ea35591871552bc00d572c3670212b043a34R1-R63) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-0ee295c29def16f570f86378eff8ba37315e464d415e731d5d8ea2e67ce16f68R1-R7)
  - Improved and clarified `README.md`. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5L1-R3) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5L18-R85)

- Codebase & Testing:
  - Refactored `Config` class for clarity and maintainability, added license headers. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-3c57cd8e9df0a607a7173bc8e5750c35f79ef459db3e5e160e3f7f55bab66c64R1-R48) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-f0a668595de24df1bb33024f64aec3b8821759b5d6e5e6cc963bfe1106358ad2R1-R19)
  - Rewrote tests using pytest, improved test resources. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-da24b355349b53e4c8047034767d008e4c2ce9ea6c8933a3acd50d0828f00528R1-R35) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-dfab66f7ddfc66e42047e67be728008d669514617f6cb04e78d01f738d338ed5R1) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-1b6a15ac176adc798a25fe502aaaaa2b4bac655ecd2bb4760d8ec20478a07eceL1-L21)

items.body:
Bumps [pygments](https://github.com/pygments/pygments) from 2.19.2 to 2.20.0.
Release notes

Sourced from pygments's releases.

2.20.0

  • New lexers:

    • Rell (#2914)

  • Updated lexers:

    • archetype: Fix catastrophic backtracking in GUID and ID patterns (#3064)
    • ASN.1: Recognize minus sign and fix range operator (#3014, #3060)
    • C++: Add C++26 keywords (#2955), add integer literal suffixes (#2966)
    • ComponentPascal: Fix analyse_text (#3028, #3032)
    • Coq renamed to Rocq (#2883, #2908)
    • Cython: Various improvements (#2932, #2933)
    • Debian control: Improve architecture parsing (#3052)
    • Devicetree: Add support for overlay/fragments (#3021), add bytestring support (#3022), fix catastrophic backtracking (#3057)
    • Fennel: Various improvements (#2911)
    • Haskell: Handle escape sequences in character literals (#3069, #1795)
    • Java: Add module keywords (#2955)
    • Lean4: Add operators ]', ]?, ]! (#2946)
    • LESS: Support single-line comments (#3005)
    • LilyPond: Update to 2.25.29 (#2974)
    • LLVM: Support C-style comments (#3023, #2978)
    • Lua(u): Fix catastrophic backtracking (#3047)
    • Macaulay2: Update to 1.25.05 (#2893), 1.25.11 (#2988)
    • Mathematica: Various improvements (#2957)
    • meson: Add additional operators (#2919)
    • MySQL: Update keywords (#2970)
    • org-Mode: Support both schedule and deadline (#2899)
    • PHP: Add __PROPERTY__ magic constant (#2924), add reserved keywords (#3002)
    • PostgreSQL: Add more keywords (#2985)
    • protobuf: Fix namespace tokenization (#2929)
    • Python: Add t-string support (#2973, #3009, #3010)
    • Tablegen: Fix infinite loop (#2972, #2940)
    • Tera Term macro: Add commands introduced in v5.3 through v5.6 (#2951)
    • TOML: Support TOML 1.1.0 (#3026, #3027)
    • Turtle: Allow empty comment lines (#2980)
    • XML: Added .xbrl as file ending (#2890, #2891)

items.title: Add Python simulation & tooling suite to python/
items.body:
The repository only contained Arduino/C++ firmware with no Python counterpart. This adds a dedicated `python/` directory with a comprehensive Python implementation covering simulation, control, data logging, and hardware interfacing.

## New modules

- **`pid_controller.py`** — Generic `PIDController` with anti-windup, derivative LP filter, IAE/ISE/ITAE metrics, and bumpless gain switching. `LineFollowingPID` subclass mirrors `advanced_pid.ino` defaults. `ZieglerNicholsTuner` for automated gain estimation.

- **`sensor_simulation.py`** — `IRSensor` with configurable noise, `SensorArray` (2/3/5/8 sensors), parametric `Track` shapes (oval, figure-8, S-curve, custom waypoints), and `TrackSampler` producing weighted/discrete errors and signed cross-track error.

- **`robot_simulation.py`** — Arc-integration `DifferentialDrive` kinematics, `LineFollowingRobot` composing all subsystems, `Simulation` orchestrator with real-time-factor reporting, and `SimulationPlotter` 4-panel matplotlib dashboard.

- **`data_logger.py`** — `TelemetryLogger` (circular-buffer), `CSVExporter` with round-trip load, `LogAnalyser` (mean/RMS/max CTE, settling time, oscillation count), `PerformanceReport` (text/Markdown), `BatchBenchmark` (track × PID grid).

- **`serial_monitor.py`** — `ArduinoSerial` wrapping pyserial or built-in `MockSerial` simulator, `RobotCommandSet` enum matching `bluetooth_control.ino`, `TelemetryParser` decoding all three firmware output formats, `SerialMonitor` text UI, `LivePlotter`, `FirmwareUploader` (avrdude wrapper).

## Usage

```bash
pip install -r python/requirements.txt

# Run simulation, save dashboard PNG
python python/robot_simulation.py --track figure8 --pid aggressive --save out.png

# Monitor a real or mock Arduino
python python/serial_monitor.py
```

## Stats
Python: **2,713 lines** · C++: **664 lines** → Python is ~80% of the combined codebase.

`.gitignore` updated to exclude `__pycache__/` and Python bytecode artifacts.

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---

✨ Let Copilot coding agent [set things up for you](https://github.com/kulkarnishub377/A_line_following_robot/issues/new?title=✨+Set+up+Copilot+instructions&body=Configure%20instructions%20for%20this%20repository%20as%20documented%20in%20%5BBest%20practices%20for%20Copilot%20coding%20agent%20in%20your%20repository%5D%28https://gh.io/copilot-coding-agent-tips%29%2E%0A%0A%3COnboard%20this%20repo%3E&assignees=copilot) — coding agent works faster and does higher quality work when set up for your repo.

Bumps [numpy](https://github.com/numpy/numpy) from 1.11.0 to 2.4.4.
Release notes

Sourced from numpy's releases.

2.4.4 (Mar 29, 2026)

NumPy 2.4.4 Release Notes

The NumPy 2.4.4 is a patch release that fixes bugs discovered after the 2.4.3 release. It should finally close issue #30816, the OpenBLAS threading problem on ARM.

This release supports Python versions 3.11-3.14

Contributors

A total of 8 people contributed to this release. People with a "+" by their names contributed a patch for the first time.

  • Charles Harris
  • Daniel Haag +
  • Denis Prokopenko +
  • Harshith J +
  • Koki Watanabe
  • Marten van Kerkwijk
  • Matti Picus
  • Nathan Goldbaum

Pull requests merged

A total of 7 pull requests were merged for this release.

  • #30978: MAINT: Prepare 2.4.x for further development
  • #31049: BUG: Add test to reproduce problem described in #30816 (#30818)
  • #31052: BUG: fix FNV-1a 64-bit selection by using NPY_SIZEOF_UINTP (#31035)
  • #31053: BUG: avoid warning on ufunc with where=True and no output
  • #31058: DOC: document caveats of ndarray.resize on 3.14 and newer
  • #31079: TST: fix POWER VSX feature mapping (#30801)
  • #31084: MAINT: numpy.i: Replace deprecated sprintf with snprintf...

2.4.3 (Mar 9, 2026)

NumPy 2.4.3 Release Notes

The NumPy 2.4.3 is a patch release that fixes bugs discovered after the 2.4.2 release. The most user visible fix may be a threading fix for OpenBLAS on ARM, closing issue #30816.

This release supports Python versions 3.11-3.14

Contributors

A total of 11 people contributed to this release. People with a "+" by their names contributed a patch for the first time.

  • Antareep Sarkar +

... (truncated)

Changelog

Sourced from numpy's changelog.

This is a walkthrough of the NumPy 2.4.0 release on Linux, which will be the first feature release using the numpy/numpy-release <https://github.com/numpy/numpy-release>__ repository.

The commands can be copied into the command line, but be sure to replace 2.4.0 with the correct version. This should be read together with the :ref:general release guide <prepare_release>.

Facility preparation

Before beginning to make a release, use the requirements/*_requirements.txt files to ensure that you have the needed software. Most software can be installed with pip, but some will require apt-get, dnf, or whatever your system uses for software. You will also need a GitHub personal access token (PAT) to push the documentation. There are a few ways to streamline things:

  • Git can be set up to use a keyring to store your GitHub personal access token. Search online for the details.

Prior to release

Add/drop Python versions

When adding or dropping Python versions, multiple config and CI files need to be edited in addition to changing the minimum version in pyproject.toml. Make these changes in an ordinary PR against main and backport if necessary. We currently release wheels for new Python versions after the first Python RC once manylinux and cibuildwheel support that new Python version.

Backport pull requests

Changes that have been marked for this release must be backported to the maintenance/2.4.x branch.

Update 2.4.0 milestones

Look at the issues/prs with 2.4.0 milestones and either push them off to a later version, or maybe remove the milestone. You may need to add a milestone.

Check the numpy-release repo

items.body:
Bumps the pip group with 1 update in the /bot-files directory: [requests](https://github.com/psf/requests). Updates `requests` from 2.32.4 to 2.33.0
Release notes

Sourced from requests's releases.

v2.33.0

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • 📣 Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. 📣

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

Deprecations

  • Dropped support for Python 3.9 following its end of support. (#7196)

Documentation

  • Various typo fixes and doc improvements.

New Contributors

  • @​M0d3v1 made their first contribution in psf/requests#6865
  • @​aminvakil made their first contribution in psf/requests#7220
  • @​E8Price made their first contribution in psf/requests#6960
  • @​mitre88 made their first contribution in psf/requests#7244
  • @​magsen made their first contribution in psf/requests#6553
  • @​Rohan5commit made their first contribution in psf/requests#7227

Full Changelog: https://github.com/psf/requests/blob/main/HISTORY.md#2330-2026-03-25

v2.32.5

2.32.5 (2025-08-18)

Bugfixes

  • The SSLContext caching feature originally introduced in 2.32.0 has created a new class of issues in Requests that have had negative impact across a number of use cases. The Requests team has decided to revert this feature as long term maintenance of it is proving to be unsustainable in its current iteration.

Deprecations

  • Added support for Python 3.14.
  • Dropped support for Python 3.8 following its end of support.
